repositories.brief_summary: Recursive-function codegen optimizations and RISC-V compressed instructions landed, alongside ongoing ephemeron GC and cross-compilation work.
repositories.new_features_summary: Recursive-function renaming optimizations, RISC-V compressed instructions, native atomic fetch-add codegen, and several typing/printast fixes.
repositories.new_features:
- Better compilation of **recursive function definitions** that avoids allocating pseudo-closure blocks for local aliases introduced before the function, addressing a code-generation regression reported in [#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876); implemented by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885)) and extended by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to cover redefinitions of identifiers from other modules ([#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894))
- **RISC-V** code generation now takes advantage of compressed instructions (the "C" ISA extension) when supported by the host processor, by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) ([#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873), [#14799](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14799))
- Native code generation for `Atomic.fetch_and_add` (`Iatomic_fetch_add`) now emits direct assembly instead of going through a C call, extended to all remaining backends (amd64, arm64, riscv, power) by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575))
- Fixed an **assertion failure** in `out_type` when external types carry a manifest subject to module constraints, by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([#14882](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14882), closes [#14881](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14881))
- Fixed **mislabeled `Pmty_extension`** in the `-dparsetree` dump, which had incorrectly printed module type extensions with the `Pmod_extension` label, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#14880](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14880))
- Fixed a **runtime memory leak**: pthread attribute objects are now correctly destroyed after use, by [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) ([#14875](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14875))
- Major GC now **adopts orphaned ephemerons early and often** (except when terminating), part of an ongoing ephemeron-simplification effort by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877), [#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813))
- Fixed a **cross-compilation build failure** on older `mingw32-gcc` toolchains caused by a missing `errno.h` include, by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) ([#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884), closes [#14883](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14883))
- More **concise printing of non-exhaustiveness warning counter-examples**, e.g. `A (_,_)` now prints as `A _`, by [Luc Maranget](https://github.com/maranget) ([#14887](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14887))
- Expect tests now also run with `-rectypes` in addition to `-principal`, exercising recursive types in more of the test suite, by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643))
repositories.activity_summary: Static linking proposed; recursive-function, Windows cross-compilation, and ephemeron GC work merged, with several typing regressions triaged.
repositories.activity:
- Newly opened work includes **static and link-time linking** support for the compiler by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) ([#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14895)), a fix for **pattern-matching switch overflow** on `abs min_int` by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#14890](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14890)), rejection of a silently-dropped extension on module-dependent functor type domains by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#14886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14886)), an overflow guard for `caml_read_section_descriptors` during **bytecode loading** by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) ([#14872](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14872)), further **ephemeron orphaning simplification** by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([#14879](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14879)), and an update to the opam instructions in `HACKING.adoc` by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) ([#14878](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14878))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a fix for a **scoping error in `ctype`** that erased result types when removing module dependencies from package types, closing [#14891](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14891) reported by [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) ([#14892](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14892))
- Merged work this week includes the **cross-compilation Windows fixes** ([#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884), [#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)), the `out_type`/external-types assertion fix ([#14882](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14882)), the `Pmty_extension` printast fix ([#14880](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14880)), the concise exhaustiveness-warning printing ([#14887](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14887)), the recursive-function renaming optimizations ([#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885), [#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894)), RISC-V compressed instructions ([#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873)), native `Iatomic_fetch_add` codegen ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)), the pthread attribute destruction fix ([#14875](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14875)), the `-rectypes` expect-test coverage ([#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)), and two ephemeron/GC PRs from [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s ongoing master plan ([#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745), [#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877))
- Ongoing, previously-opened work still under review includes [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez)'s **new GC-pacing logic** ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s `Atomic.fold_update` proposal ([#14725](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14725)) and `Dynarray.unsafe_{get,set}` ([#14864](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14864)), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)'s signature-inclusion alias handling ([#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)), [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse)'s configurable domain stack size ([#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860)) and `Dynarray.sort` ([#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848)), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)'s instance-variable-lifting fix for [#14861](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14861) ([#14868](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14868)), [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)'s memory-model documentation for `memory.c` ([#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857)), [Martin Constantino–Bodin](https://github.com/Mbodin)'s `Sys.mkdir` permissions documentation ([#14863](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14863)), and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)'s small libunix fixes ([#14740](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14740))
- Two long-dormant PRs from 2021 by [Nicolas Chataing](https://github.com/nchataing) were closed without merging: adding `floatarray` manipulation functions ([#10246](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10246)) and rewriting `get_unboxed_type_representation` with a terminating algorithm ([#10479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10479))
- Typing regressions were reported and quickly triaged: [@yannl35133](https://github.com/yannl35133)'s report of a recursive first-class-module typing regression in 5.5 ([#14888](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14888)) was closed as a duplicate/tracked issue, and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s related report ([#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630)) is being tracked by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) for a fix before the 5.6.0 release
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions covered an inlining regression since 5.2, module-type precedence, and point-free Option/Result combinators.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho)'s report of a **code-generation regression** in inlining of local closure variables since OCaml 5.2 ([#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876)) prompted an extensive benchmarking discussion with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls), and [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) (who linked the related performance-regression thread [#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733)); benchmarks with and without flambda were compared before landing the fixes in [#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885) and [#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894)
- [@314eter](https://github.com/314eter)'s question about **operator precedence** in `with module type` constraints ([#14874](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14874)) led [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) to discuss whether a breaking change is acceptable, converging on consistency with existing `with type` precedence; [@314eter](https://github.com/314eter) has a branch ready pending consensus
- [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann)'s proposal to add point-free `Option`/`Result` combinators `andthen`/`orelse` ([#14893](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14893)) was clarified by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) to be distinct from the previously-discussed and closed [#13937](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13937) proposal for `value_or`
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing ephemeron GC simplification, recursive-closure codegen work, and cross-compilation/Windows robustness improvements.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) continues a systematic, multi-PR **ephemeron orphaning/adoption simplification** effort tracked against a master plan in [#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720), landing [#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813) and [#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877) this week and opening [#14879](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14879) for further simplification
- Recursive-function and closure **code-generation optimization** work, triggered by the regression report in [#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876), is proceeding incrementally with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) each contributing targeted renaming optimizations ([#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885), [#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894))
- Continued investment in **cross-compilation and Windows build robustness**, spanning Windows toolchain fixes ([#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850), [#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884)), `BINDIR` overrides ([#14869](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14869)), and small libunix fixes ([#14740](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14740))
- Multi-architecture **native code generation parity** work, extending atomic primitives and instruction selection across amd64, arm64, RISC-V, power, and s390x ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575), [#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873))
repositories.contributors_summary: Thirteen contributors landed codegen optimizations, GC work, cross-compilation fixes, and typing bug reports/fixes, plus active review discussion.

repositories.brief_summary: The tar-library repository rewrite story (#6625) advanced through several merged PRs, alongside portability, solver, and CI fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: A new `OpamTar` library module replaced external `tar` calls, plus opam-root upgrade and parse-error reporting improvements.
repositories.new_features:
- Added an `OpamTar` module that reads and writes tar.gz archives natively via the `tar` library instead of shelling out to the `tar` command, removing an external process dependency used when creating repository archives ([#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou), original work by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate))
- Upgraded the `ocaml-tar` dependency to 3.5.0 to support the archive-handling rewrite ([#6976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6976) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate))
- Added a conditional **hard upgrade** mechanism for the opam root format upgrade process, letting most users take a lighter no-op upgrade path while only affected setups go through the heavier migration ([#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6949) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou))
- Opam file parsing errors now print the originating **repository** when the information is available, making it easier to locate malformed opam files ([#6971](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6971) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou))
- Fixed a bug where an `undefined variable` error in a lock file filter was silently ignored instead of failing gracefully in strict mode ([#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6947) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou), fixes [#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946))
- Fixed `opam config subst` so it correctly handles **absolute paths** instead of mishandling them ([#6936](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6936) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb), fixes [#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925))
- Stopped the opam 1.2-to-2.0 repository upgrade process from downloading packages without checksums just to compute a non-trusted md5, removing a step whose success depended on an external server being reachable ([#6978](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6978) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate))
- Fixed `OpamFilename.starts_with` and `dir_starts_with`, which incorrectly treated `foo/bar` as a prefix of `foo/bar-baz` and mishandled directory-vs-file prefix checks, alongside a new minimal unit-test framework for the library ([#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6953) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb), fixes [#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948))
- Updated `Packaging.md` with a paragraph explaining that static archives are preferable to forge-generated archives when manually releasing packages, since the latter can change checksums ([#6973](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6973) by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs addressed local-repository symlinks and cross-user git repos, while several PRs tied to the #6625 repository rewrite merged.
repositories.activity:
- Newly opened PRs still awaiting merge include a workaround for **symlinks in local opam repositories** that broke CI caching by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([#6977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6977)), support for using **git repositories owned by another local user** by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6980), fixes [#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963)), and a draft fix so `/` is recognised as a directory separator on **Windows** when parsing command arguments, also by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6981](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6981), fixes [#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940))
- The ongoing **repository-format rewrite** underlying [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6625) (replacing tree-scanning updates with tar.gz diffing) progressed with several supporting PRs merging this week: the `OpamTar` module ([#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945)), the conditional hard-upgrade mechanism ([#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6949)), the repository-aware parse-error messages ([#6971](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6971)), the `ocaml-tar` 3.5.0 bump ([#6976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6976)), and assorted reftest fixups ([#6970](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6970))
- CI maintenance merged this week included fixing the **Cygwin backend** basic tests ([#6979](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6979) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)), fixing depexts jobs on Ubuntu and openSUSE ([#6960](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6960) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)), and bumping `actions/checkout` from 6 to 7 ([#6969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6969) via Dependabot)
- Ongoing review continued on conditional git-config application for `opam source --dev`, where [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl) iterated on `full_fetch` semantics based on feedback from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6955), fixes [#6451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6451))
- [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz) continued work on skipping default **compiler selection** for `opam init --bare`, showing a measured startup-time improvement in the PR description ([#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6957), fixes [#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815))
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)'s proposed `--ignore-available-on` argument for ignoring package availability conditions remains under discussion ([#6836](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6836), fixes [#5283](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5283))
- [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio)'s dead-code cleanup removing unused exported API elements continues to be scoped down with [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), who prefers to pick-and-choose individual removals rather than merge the full set ([#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6954))
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) also opened an exhaustive reftest for all `.install` file fields, still in review ([#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6956))
repositories.discussions_summary: A user questioned why opam's solver proposed downgrading ansifmt while upgrading re, calling it a breaking-change solution.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [lexa](https://github.com/qexat) asked why `opam update && opam upgrade` proposed downgrading `ansifmt` from 2.0.0 to 0.3.0 as part of an upgrade to `re` 1.14.0, noting the downgrade would break dependents; the discussion was marked answered ([#6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966))
repositories.trends_summary: Development continued shifting external-process calls (tar, compiler probing) into native OCaml code, alongside expanded reftest and unit-test tooling.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Replacing shell-outs with native OCaml libraries: the tar-handling rewrite ([#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945), [#6976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6976)) feeding into [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6625), and skipping unnecessary compiler-selection work in `opam init --bare` ([#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6957))
- Growing investment in test infrastructure, including a new unit-test framework for `OpamFilename` ([#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6953)), an exhaustive `.install` field reftest ([#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6956)), and repository-layout reftests ([#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6941))
- Continued portability fixes for Windows path/separator handling ([#6981](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6981), [#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940)) and for local repositories containing symlinks ([#6977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6977))
- Recurring solver-behavior questions from users around dependency downgrades and cyclic upgrade/downgrade loops ([#6951](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6951), [#6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966))
repositories.issues_summary: An open issue tagged 'easy first issue' covers clarifying opam's error message for globally-set-up repositories.
repositories.good_first_issues: - Improve the error message shown when adding a repository whose name is already globally set up under a different URL, so it's clearer that the mapping is global and where it was configured; a maintainer has invited a PR for this ([#6943](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6943) by [@yannl35133](https://github.com/yannl35133), labeled "easy first issue")
repositories.contributors_summary: kit-ty-kate and R. Boujbel drove the tar-based repository rewrite; NathanReb, dra27-js, sporkl, tuesdayjz, mseri, WardBrian, and fantazio also contributed.
repositories.contributors:
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) merged the `ocaml-tar` upgrade ([#6976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6976)), the Cygwin CI fix ([#6979](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6979)), and the checksum-download fix for the 1.2-to-2.0 upgrade path ([#6978](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6978)); opened the Windows directory-separator fix ([#6981](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6981)) and git-repository-ownership support ([#6980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6980)); filed an issue about the opam-switch-import invariant ([#6982](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6982)); and provided detailed review feedback on [#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6955), [#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6953), [#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6957), [#6977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6977), [#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6941), [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6956), and [#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6954)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) merged the conditional hard-upgrade mechanism ([#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6949)), the repository-aware parse-error messages ([#6971](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6971)), reftest fixups ([#6970](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6970)), the repository-layout reftest ([#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6941)), and the depexts CI fixes ([#6960](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6960)); opened the lock-file undefined-variable fix ([#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6947)) and the `.install` exhaustive reftest ([#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6956)); filed an issue about the sandbox flag not propagating on opam-root reinit ([#6968](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6968)); and reviewed [#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6953) and [#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6955)
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) fixed `opam config subst` absolute-path handling ([#6936](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6936)) and `OpamFilename` prefix-matching bugs together with a new unit-test framework ([#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6953))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) opened a symlink workaround for local opam repositories ([#6977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6977)) and, as [dra27](https://github.com/dra27), filed an issue requesting the ability to demote lint errors to warnings ([#6964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6964))
- [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl) continued iterating on conditional git-config application for `opam source --dev` based on review feedback ([#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6955))
- [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz) opened a fix to skip compiler selection for `opam init --bare`, including before/after timing measurements ([#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6957))
- [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) merged a `Packaging.md` documentation update about preferring static archives ([#6973](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6973))
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) continued discussion on the proposed `--ignore-available-on` argument ([#6836](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6836))
- [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio) continued the dead-code cleanup PR removing unused exported API elements, discussing scope with maintainers ([#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6954))
- [@yannl35133](https://github.com/yannl35133) reported a misleading error message for repositories already set up under a different URL ([#6943](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6943))
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported that stale state caches after an opam version upgrade slow down `--safe` operations and editor startup ([#6975](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6975))
- [Pierre Villemot](https://github.com/Halbaroth) reported that a wrongly formatted name in `pin-depends` is silently ignored by `opam switch create` ([#6952](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6952))
- [Vasile Rotaru](https://github.com/vrotaru) reported a solver cyclic upgrade/downgrade loop and supplied a switch-export reproduction file that [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) used to reproduce the issue ([#6951](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6951))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) reported that opam's internal `git ls-files` call fails when the git clone is owned by a different user than the one running opam, which [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) addressed in [#6980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6980) ([#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963))
- [lexa](https://github.com/qexat) opened a Q&A discussion questioning the solver's proposed downgrade of `ansifmt` ([#6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966))

repositories.brief_summary: Release 1.27.0 shipped with an OCaml 5.5 upgrade and a fix for a long-standing `-short-paths` merlin configuration issue.
repositories.new_features_summary: Merlin now defaults to `real_paths` instead of forcing `-short-paths`, and the server gained OCaml 5.5 support.
repositories.new_features:
- Merlin configuration no longer forces `real_paths = false`, letting the underlying Merlin default (`real_paths = true` from `Mconfig.initial`) take effect so type errors can show full, non-shortened paths for type aliases; fixed by [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) and closes the issue reported by [Guillaume Bertholon](https://github.com/GBertholon) ([#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579), [#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1395))
- Added support for the **OCaml 5.5** compiler, merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) as part of the 1.27.0 release preparation ([#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609))
repositories.activity_summary: Release 1.27.0 was published, bundling the OCaml 5.5 upgrade, and the 550-preview PR was folded into that release work.
repositories.activity:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) merged the **OCaml 5.5 upgrade** and used it to prepare and publish the **1.27.0 release**, which also bundles the `-short-paths` fix and the earlier `Stdune.Pid` compatibility fix ([#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609))
- The **"550 preview"** branch opened by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) was closed without merging after its changes were folded into the OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR ([#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1597), [#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609))
- The **merlin configuration** fix for `-short-paths` was merged, closing an issue open since November 2024 about type-alias display in error messages ([#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579), [#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1395))
repositories.discussions_summary: Brief review exchange on the merlin `-short-paths` fix between the author and maintainer.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) pinged [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) for review on the `-short-paths` fix, noting it should backport cleanly to 414-LTS; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) thanked them and merged it, apologizing for the review delay ([#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued alignment with upstream OCaml compiler releases and refinement of default Merlin configuration behavior.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Prompt tracking of upstream **OCaml compiler releases**, with the 5.5 upgrade merged and released within days of being opened ([#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609))
- Revisiting long-standing **default configuration choices** passed to Merlin (e.g. `real_paths`) to better match expected compiler-like behavior ([#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579), [#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1395))
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors were involved: two authored merged fixes, one reported the underlying issue, and one had a preview PR superseded.
repositories.contributors:
- [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) authored and merged the fix for the merlin `-short-paths` configuration default ([#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579)), closing the issue reported by [Guillaume Bertholon](https://github.com/GBertholon)
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) merged the OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR, reviewed and merged the `-short-paths` fix, and published the 1.27.0 release ([#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609), [#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579))
- [Guillaume Bertholon](https://github.com/GBertholon) reported the `-short-paths` configuration issue that was resolved this week ([#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1395))
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened the "550 preview" PR, later closed after its changes were incorporated into the OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR ([#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1597))

repositories.brief_summary: Merlin shipped release v5.8-505 with OCaml 5.5 support, alongside continued review of an indexing memory-usage PR and several LSP/dune discrepancy discussions.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.5 support landed and release v5.8-505 was published bundling signature-help and locate fixes.
repositories.new_features:
- **OCaml 5.5 support** was merged into the merlin library by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077), fixes [#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024))
- Release **v5.8-505** was published by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), bundling the OCaml 5.5 support along with a fix for **signature-help with type aliases** ([#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067), fixes [#1927](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1927)) and a fix for **locate on punned let bindings** so it resolves to the identifier rather than the pattern ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) ([#2083](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2083))
- Test suite cleanup removed a stale FIXME for [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1404) and added two regression tests, included in the v5.8-505 changelog ([#2073](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2073))
- A **cram test** was disabled on FreeBSD in the `typer-cache` test suite to unblock CI on that platform ([7eccdda](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/7eccdda5dcd8e31c1c56cbcc268eac844f331963))
repositories.activity_summary: Dependabot opened a checkout-action bump while review continued on an indexing memory-usage PR and an ocamlgrep integration attempt was closed.
repositories.activity:
- **Dependency maintenance PR** opened by [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) to bump `actions/checkout` from 6 to 7, still awaiting merge at week's end ([#2081](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2081))
- Release engineering work by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) landed the **v5.8-505 release**, updating `CHANGES.md` and merging the OCaml 5.5 upgrade branch into main ([#2083](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2083), [#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077))
- Ongoing review of an **LRU cache for indexing memory usage** continued, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) addressing feedback from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) on storing small values alongside their parent, making filenames relative to the indexer's working directory, and disabling related-uids compression; the PR builds on earlier work in [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079))
- The **ocamlgrep structural-search integration** proposed by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) was closed without merging after discussion with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) about whether the cmt-scanning approach belonged in the merlin repository ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064))
- A long-standing **dependency bump** for `cachix/install-nix-action` remains open, tracking upstream releases up to 31.10.6 ([#2065](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2065))
- Issue triage by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) closed a duplicate report of an **error discrepancy between dune build and ocaml-lsp** ([#1965](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1965)) while moving the underlying ppx-path-resolution issue to merlin for further investigation ([#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082))
- [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def)'s long-open request for **path remapping for Docker & Tramp** was revisited, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) asking whether it is still needed now that the LSP protocol has matured ([#611](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/611))
- A new bug was reported by [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) describing that **renaming a type in a signature does not propagate to the implementation** when using the occurrences-based rename command ([#2084](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2084))
- [François Bérenger](https://github.com/UnixJunkie)'s older request for `merlin-occurrences-expr` remains open, with [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) confirming it is still reproducible and planning to investigate ([#372](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/372))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions covered the root cause of a dune/ocaml-lsp ppx path mismatch and truncated error messages in merlin.el.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) identified the likely root cause of the **dune build vs ocaml-lsp error discrepancy for ppx expressions**: Dune runs ppxes from `_build/default` while Merlin starts them from the source tree root, suggesting Merlin needs more build-system information to start ppxes from the correct directory ([#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) debated why **merlin.el truncates error messages** to their last line, discussing whether the "footnote" field in Merlin's error payload should be used at all, and whether the fix belongs in `merlin.el` or in the better-maintained `ocaml-eglot` client ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) discussed the tradeoffs of integrating **ocamlgrep** directly into merlin versus keeping it a separate project, noting a potential future benefit from a shared cmt cache ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) asked whether the seven-year-old **path remapping for Docker & Tramp** request is still relevant given LSP protocol improvements, potentially heading toward closure ([#611](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/611))
repositories.trends_summary: Recurring focus on indexer memory/performance work and on reconciling path-resolution assumptions between dune and merlin's LSP-facing tooling.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Continued investment in **indexing memory usage**, with the LRU cache PR building directly on prior work to reduce indexer footprint ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079), [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050))
- Repeated surfacing of **path and working-directory assumptions** mismatched between dune and merlin/ocaml-lsp, spanning ppx execution ([#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082), [#1965](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1965)) and remote/containerized development setups ([#611](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/611))
- Editor-client error rendering differences (merlin.el vs ocaml-eglot) prompting discussion about where truncation fixes belong ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080))
- External tooling contributions, such as the ocamlgrep structural-search proposal, being evaluated for fit within the merlin project rather than merged outright ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064))
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue tackles truncated error messages shown by merlin.el.
repositories.good_first_issues: - Fix **truncated error messages** displayed by merlin.el on OCaml 5.5, where only the last line of a multi-part type error is shown instead of the full compiler output, reported by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080))
repositories.contributors_summary: voodoos led release engineering and indexer review work while others reported bugs and discussed editor-integration issues.
repositories.contributors:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) merged the OCaml 5.5 upgrade and published release v5.8-505 ([#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077), [#2083](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2083)), continued iterating on the LRU cache indexing PR in response to review feedback ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)), responded to the closed ocamlgrep integration proposal ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)), triaged and closed a duplicate dune/ocaml-lsp issue while moving the underlying report to merlin ([#1965](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1965), [#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082)), and followed up on the long-open Docker/Tramp path-remapping request ([#611](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/611))
- [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) opened a dependency bump for `actions/checkout` ([#2081](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2081)) and maintains an open bump for `cachix/install-nix-action` ([#2065](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2065))
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) reviewed the LRU cache indexing PR, prompting several follow-up commits from [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079))
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) authored the ocamlgrep structural-search integration proposal and discussed its scope with maintainers ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) reported the truncated error message issue and participated in the discussion of its root cause ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080))
- [@xvw](https://github.com/xvw) participated in discussions on the truncated error message issue ([#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)) and confirmed reproducibility of the `merlin-occurrences-expr` request ([#372](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/372))
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported a bug where renaming a type in a signature does not update its implementation ([#2084](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2084))
- [François Bérenger](https://github.com/UnixJunkie) is the original reporter of the open `merlin-occurrences-expr` feature request ([#372](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/372))
- [Raoul Hidalgo Charman](https://github.com/RaoulHC) reported the dune/ocaml-lsp error discrepancy issues that were triaged this week ([#1965](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1965), [#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082))

repositories.brief_summary: Shell completion landed, `--auto-promote`/`dune fmt` now exit 0 on success, and watch-mode/RPC build reporting got several correctness fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: Command-line shell completion, zero-exit `--auto-promote`, lazy `blang` boolean evaluation, and file-watch debouncing shipped this week.
repositories.new_features:
- Added `dune` command, subcommand, and flag completion for `bash`, `zsh`, and `pwsh` via `dune completion [shell] --help` by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) ([#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14779)), with a matching man page wired into the build ([doc/dune.inc](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/commit/8dd0d9a4191143789b93f87b39804f30da9406d0)) and `dune promote <TAB>` completion against the `.to-promote` database opened by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([#15338](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15338))
- `dune build --auto-promote` and `dune fmt` now exit with status **0** once all promotions are applied instead of forcing callers to wrap the command in `|| true`, fixed by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15284](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15284), fixes [#785](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/785))
- `blang` boolean expressions (`and`/`or` in `enabled_if` and similar fields) are now short-circuited and evaluated lazily by default, contributed by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) ([#15291](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15291))
- File-change events in watch mode are now debounced before triggering a rebuild, reducing redundant rebuild churn, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15344](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15344))
- `$ dune rpc build` requests in watch mode now complete as soon as their own build finishes rather than waiting on unrelated concurrent RPC build requests, and exit with status 1 when the requested build fails, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15191](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15191), [#15341](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15341))
- RPC build-progress reporting now shows rules built against the total number of *live* rules rather than rules executed against rules discovered in the current run, affecting both the status line and progress reported over RPC, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) with [Andrey Mokhov](https://github.com/snowleopard) and [@pwhite](https://github.com/pwhite) ([#14985](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14985))
- `dune clean` now accepts source directories (and the project root), not just paths inside `_build`, mirroring how `dune build PATH` already works, opened by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15305](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15305), fixes [#2288](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/2288))
- `dune describe env` (`show env`) now accepts absolute paths that point inside the workspace instead of rejecting them as external, opened by [Pushkar Singh](https://github.com/pushkarscripts) ([#15296](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15296), fixes [#15104](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15104))
- Odoc documentation rules are now sandboxed by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15190](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15190))
- Fixed watch mode on Windows by creating the file-watcher sync directory before use, avoiding an `open(_build/.sync\0): No such file or directory` error when an RPC client requests a build, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#14678](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14678), fixes [#14632](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14632))
- Fixed watch-mode status lines so Dune shows a restart status while cancelling a build interrupted by source changes and resumes the normal progress counter on the next build, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15289](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15289), fixes [#11134](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11134))
- Fixed the `%{ppx:ppx1+..+ppxn}` form, which previously pointed at the wrong binary, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15361))
- Cram tests that time out now show partial output instead of none, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15269](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15269), related to [#12343](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12343))
- Dune now cleans up build processes it spawned before starting a fresh build on Linux, closing escaped subreaper children on shutdown, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15239](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15239), [#15274](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15274), [#15332](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15332))
- Documented the `(corrections <setting>)` rule field, letting custom rules that rewrite, format, or lint source files opt in to `.corrected`-file promotion discovery without an explicit `(diff? ...)` action, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15365](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15365))
- Clarified `dune install` directory-layout precedence in the docs: `--prefix` follows the opam layout (`man`/`doc` directly under the prefix, not `share`) and takes precedence over directories configured at `./configure` time, with corrected `--libdir`/`--bindir`/etc. flag names and an FHS-layout recipe, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15306](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15306), addresses [#5733](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5733))
- Corrected the `foreign_library`/`foreign_stubs` docs from the non-existent `(include_dir ...)` to the actual `(include_dirs ...)` field name, and documented `(disable_dynamically_linked_foreign_archives true)`, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15301](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15301))
repositories.activity_summary: Twelve PRs opened including nix-completion and Rocq test-naming work, alongside ongoing RPC/watch-mode hardening and a large-scale close-out of long-standing issues.
repositories.activity:
- Twelve **new PRs opened** this week and not yet merged, including a linked-cancellation primitive for the fiber library by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15364](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15364)), an odoc `@doc-new` fallback fix for build-dir `cmti` paths by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([#15293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15293)), Rocq output-test naming and directory-attachment fixes by [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) ([#15349](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15349), [#15347](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15347)), and CI/nix cleanups by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([#15326](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15326), [#15327](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15327))
- The **RPC and watch-mode subsystem** saw an extended refactoring push by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg): client lifecycle and status tracking were reworked ([#15345](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15345), [#15346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15346), [#15330](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15330)), long-polling requests are now cancelled when sessions close ([#15362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15362), [#15363](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15363)), build requests were moved into the core build system ([#15328](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15328), [#15329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15329), [#15333](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15333), [#15348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15348)), and the background RPC wrapper was removed in favor of a simpler client signature ([#15270](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15270), [#15358](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15358))
- The **nix development shell** was substantially reorganized by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), splitting the monolithic `flake.nix` into focused modules for Rocq ([#15318](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15318), [#15314](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15314)), OxCaml ([#15312](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15312), [#15309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15309), [#15311](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15311), [#15310](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15310)), and the dune package itself ([#15302](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15302), [#15303](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15303), [#15304](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15304), [#15307](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15307), [#15308](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15308)), plus enabling shell completion inside the dev shell itself ([#15337](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15337)) and adding an `extraOCamlOverlays` parameter ([#15313](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15313))

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion continued on the `ld.conf` stublibs warning issue with a proposed patch to normalize paths via `Fl_split.norm_dir` (#115).
repositories.discussions_summary: Contributors debated the cause and a candidate fix for the repeated ocamlfind stublibs warning under OCaml 5.5 (#115).
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) confirmed reproducing the "Cannot read directory ../stublibs which is mentioned in ld.conf" warning reported by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), with a typical repro using `opam switch create . 5.5.0` followed by `opam install ocamlfind` and `ocamlfind ocamlc -version` ([#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
- [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) proposed a patch to `src/findlib/frontend.ml` that normalizes relative paths read from `ld.conf` to absolute paths in `read_ldconf`, while noting that comparing paths via `Fl_split.norm_dir` may not be reliable enough since it does not resolve `.` and `..` segments, and suggested comparing via `Unix.stat` device/inode numbers instead ([#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
repositories.contributors_summary: Activity centered on the open `ld.conf` warning issue, with reports, reproduction, and a proposed fix from three contributors.
repositories.contributors:
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened and maintained the discussion on the ocamlfind stublibs warning under OCaml 5.5, cc'ing [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) for input ([#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
- [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) confirmed the issue was also reproducible on their machine and shared a minimal repro sequence ([#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
- [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) analyzed the root cause in `Fl_split.norm_dir` and `read_ldconf`, and shared a draft patch normalizing paths read from `ld.conf` to absolute form ([#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion opened on odoc_driver resolving sibling-library module names incorrectly for Eliom's client/server split; CI dependency bump merged.
repositories.activity_summary: Dependabot merged a CI actions/checkout update while maintainers began investigating a module-resolution report for Eliom.
repositories.activity:
- Routine dependency maintenance merged: [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) updated **actions/checkout** from v6 to v7 in the CI workflow configuration, a two-file change limited to `.github/workflows/build.yml` and `.github/workflows/oxcaml.yml` ([#1451](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1451)).
- Investigation opened into **odoc_driver's module resolution** for packages with sibling libraries that intentionally share module names, specifically Eliom's `eliom.client` and `eliom.server` (used for the Ocsigen framework's client/server symmetry), with [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) proposing the `{!/eliom.client/Module}` reference syntax as a partial workaround for hand-written doc comments and planning to reproduce the problem by building docs for eliom with odoc_driver ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450)).
repositories.discussions_summary: Vincent Balat raised a design problem in odoc_driver's link-order resolution affecting packages with same-named sibling library modules.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) opened [#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450) describing how odoc_driver resolves same-named modules from sibling libraries to the wrong library's definitions, using Eliom's `eliom.client` and `eliom.server` as the motivating case (the client's `Content.Html` was showing the server's definitions); he traced the cause to two factors, same-named modules being picked by first-found order during linking and canonical paths ending up pointing at the sibling library, and mentioned a patched fork that links each unit only against its own declared dependencies, which fixes Eliom but would remove the package-wide reference availability odoc_driver otherwise provides.
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) responded suggesting the `{!/eliom.client/Module}` disambiguation syntax, and after [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) clarified that the incorrect resolution occurs in automatically generated API content rather than hand-written references, confirmed the problem reproduces when building Eliom's documentation with odoc_driver on ocaml.org and said he would look further into the cause ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450)).
repositories.contributors_summary: Vincent Balat reported and characterized a module-resolution bug affecting Eliom's client/server libraries, which Jon Ludlam began investigating.
repositories.contributors:
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reported a module-resolution problem affecting packages with sibling libraries that share module names on purpose, using his work on Ocsigen/Eliom documentation tooling (the `wodoc` layer built on top of odoc_driver) as context, provided a detailed technical writeup of the two contributing causes, and shared a link to his own patched fork that works around the issue locally ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450)).
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) reviewed the report, suggested a reference-syntax workaround for hand-written doc comments, and after learning that workaround did not cover the automatically resolved API content, confirmed he could reproduce the problem via odoc_driver's build of Eliom on ocaml.org and committed to investigating further ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450)).

repositories.brief_summary: Watch.ocaml.org scraper made resilient to failures; PRs advanced for AWS industrial users, GC tutorial fix, and sqlite3 FTS5 example.
repositories.new_features_summary: Watch.ocaml.org video scraper now isolates source failures instead of aborting the whole Planet scrape job.
repositories.new_features:
- The `watch.ocaml.org` **video scraper** (`tool/data-scrape/lib/watch_scraper.ml`) now wraps its fetch in the same per-source `try/with` pattern already used by the blog and YouTube scrapers: a network outage, expired TLS certificate, or malformed response is caught, logged as a `[WARN]`, and reported as a non-fatal `Scrape_report.Error`, leaving the existing `data/video-watch.yml` untouched rather than raising an uncaught exception that aborted the entire scheduled job, by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695))
- Bumped the `actions/checkout` GitHub Action from v6 to v7 across all CI workflows (`ci.yml`, `debug-ci.yml`, `markdown-lint.yml`, `release-scrapers.yml`, `scrape.yml`, `scrape_platform_releases.yml`) ([#3691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3691))
- Updated the LexiFi job listing's publication date to refresh a fresh recruitment campaign, by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) ([#3701](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3701))
- Added the platform newsletter covering February-May 2026 to the site's data, by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) ([#3680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3680))
- Automated **Planet blog aggregation** and **platform release** scrapes pulled in new posts from anil, tunbury, taride, cwn, ocsigen, bbatsov and jonludlam, plus updated platform release data ([#3677](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3677), [#3692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3692), [#3697](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3697))

- [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) merged the **watch.ocaml.org scraper resilience fix** ([#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695)), closing the incident report ([#3694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3694)), and opened a follow-up issue tracking remaining hardening: decoupling the `planet`/`video` make steps and isolating non-network scraper failures such as config-decode and YAML-serialization errors ([#3696](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3696))
- Routine merges included the **LexiFi job listing refresh** by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) ([#3701](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3701)), the **platform newsletter data addition** by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) with review feedback on date formatting ([#3680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3680)), the **actions/checkout v7 bump** via dependabot ([#3691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3691)), and automated **Planet/platform-release scrapes** ([#3677](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3677), [#3692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3692), [#3697](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3697))
- [Lemonsity](https://github.com/Lemonsity) reported that the Emacs tooling setup guide binds `eglot-ensure` to the now-obsolete `ocaml-eglot-hook` instead of `ocaml-eglot-mode-hook`, triggering an aliasing warning in Emacs 30.2 ([#3700](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3700))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion covered a site outage, the scraper-resilience incident's root cause, and how to preserve original RWO tutorial text.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Rafał](https://github.com/rafrafek) reported ocaml.org was down and, once it recovered, asked whether the project uses any uptime monitor, suggesting StatusCake or Pulsetic as free options ([#3699](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3699))
- [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) traced the OCaml Planet scrape job failure to an expired `watch.ocaml.org` TLS certificate causing an uncaught exception, noted the blog scraper already isolated per-source failures while the video scraper did not, and outlined the asymmetry resolved by [#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695) ([#3694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3694))
- On the **GC tutorial PR**, [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) and [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) discussed keeping Anil Madhavapeddy's and Yaron Minsky's original Real World OCaml text unchanged while adding a preamble note and appendix with `Stdlib` equivalents for `Core`-dependent examples ([#3682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3682))
repositories.trends_summary: Per-source failure isolation is spreading across the scraper codebase, following a pattern from an outage-triggered incident.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **per-source error isolation** pattern pioneered in the blog scraper is being extended to other scrapers after an outage exposed the gap in `watch.ocaml.org` handling, with further hardening (decoupling make steps, catching non-network failures) already tracked as follow-up work ([#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695), [#3696](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3696))
- Community-contributed **content and cookbook examples** continue arriving from outside the core team, including a sqlite3 FTS5 search example ([#3698](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3698)) and an industrial-users entry for AWS ([#3681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3681))
- Automated `[scrape]` and dependency-bump PRs from `github-actions` and `dependabot` continue at a steady cadence alongside manual content work ([#3677](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3677), [#3691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3691), [#3692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3692), [#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693), [#3697](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3697), [#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702))
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue remains open on replacing ASCII-only string comparisons with Unicode-aware Uucp functions.
repositories.good_first_issues: - Replace case-insensitive string comparisons based on `String.lowercase_ascii` and structural equality with i18n-aware `Uucp` `caselesseq` functions across the ocaml.org codebase, labeled good first issue/beginner/help wanted, with [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) outlining a plan to locate and migrate the affected call sites ([#2444](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/2444), related to [#2442](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/2442))
repositories.contributors_summary: Cuihtlauac Alvarado fixed and tracked scraper resilience; adith nk, Jones Melton, Nicolás Ojeda Bär and sabine advanced content PRs; others filed issues and reviews.
repositories.contributors:
- [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) implemented and merged the **watch.ocaml.org scraper resilience fix** ([#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695)), documented the triggering incident and closed it once resolved ([#3694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3694)), opened a follow-up hardening issue ([#3696](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3696)), and discussed the approach for preserving original tutorial text on the GC tutorial PR ([#3682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3682))
- [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) opened the **AWS industrial-users PR** ([#3681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3681), fixing [#3652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3652)) and the **GC tutorial Core-dependency clarification** ([#3682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3682)), and commented on the good first issue for Uucp-based caseless string comparison with an implementation plan ([#2444](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/2444))
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) merged the **LexiFi job listing refresh** ([#3701](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3701))
- [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) contributed and merged the **platform newsletter data addition**, suggesting date-format corrections during review ([#3680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3680))
- [Jones Melton](https://github.com/jonesmelton) opened a **sqlite3 FTS5 search cookbook example** using the thin `sqlite3-ocaml` binding ([#3698](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3698))
- [Juneyoung Lee](https://github.com/aqjune) opened the issue requesting AWS be listed as an industrial user and reviewed/approved the resulting PR ([#3652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3652), [#3681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3681))
- [Lemonsity](https://github.com/Lemonsity) reported an outdated Emacs LSP setup instruction referencing an obsolete variable alias ([#3700](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3700))
- [Rafał](https://github.com/rafrafek) reported a site outage and raised the question of adopting an uptime monitor ([#3699](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3699))

repositories.brief_summary: New packages soteria, playwright, osx-keychain and forcamla landed alongside dune 3.24.0, the OCaml 5.5.0 manual, and melange 7.0.0.
repositories.new_features_summary: Four new packages, dune 3.24.0, OCaml 5.5.0 manual and toolchain support, melange 7.0.0, and arm32 support for core.

- New package **playwright** OCaml bindings by [Dmytro Melnychuk](https://github.com/dmytro-melno) ([#30146](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30146))
- New package **osx-keychain** by [Alex Leighton](https://github.com/alexleighton) ([#30148](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30148))

repositories.discussions_summary: A tangled.org archive-generation change broke wol package checksums, prompting a cross-maintainer discussion on source-archive policy.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Issue [#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089) tracked a bad checksum on the **wol** packages traced to a change in how the tangled.org forge generates git-archive tarballs; [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported the mismatch, [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) and [akshay](https://github.com/oppiliappan) (tangled maintainer) diagnosed the cause, [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) cited the repository policy against modifying source-archive checksums, and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) discussed publishing a static, annotated-tag archive instead — resolved by recovering the tarball into opam-source-archives ([#30126](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30126))
- Issue [#30121](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30121) discussed whether OCaml "trunk" compiler variant packages that intentionally omit checksums (e.g. `ocaml-variants.5.1.1+flambda2+trunk`) should be archived, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) concluding most flagged packages are git-branch sources that don't need archiving
repositories.trends_summary: Dune 3.24 required downstream upper-bound pins, and recurring source-archive breakage drove repeated cache-recovery fixes this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Dune version-bound fallout**: the 3.24.0 release ([#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) required immediate upper-bound pins on cppo, lablgtk3, wasm_of_ocaml-compiler ([#30143](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30143)) and camomile ([#30132](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30132)) to keep existing packages installable
- **Recurring archive breakage**: several packages lost their upstream source tarballs to 404s or checksum drift this week ([#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089), [#30104](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30104), [#30110](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30110), [#30112](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30112), [#30114](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30114), [#30115](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30115), [#30116](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30116), [#30122](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30122)), each resolved by re-hosting the tarball in opam-source-archives ([#30126](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30126), [#30127](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30127), [#30128](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30128), [#30129](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30129), [#30102](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30102), [#30103](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30103))
- **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem readiness**: the manual, merlin and ocaml-lsp-server all shipped 5.5 support in the same week ([#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095), [#30117](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30117)), following the macOS install issue reported against the 5.5.0~alpha compiler ([#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
