repositories.brief_summary: New Crypto, Notification and Clipboard Web API bindings merged alongside a test-infrastructure overhaul built on ppx_expect_light.
repositories.new_features_summary: Crypto, Notification, Clipboard bindings and Lwt MutationObserver support merged; lwt_log removed and a wasm dune 3.24 path fix landed.
repositories.new_features:
- New `Js_of_ocaml.Crypto` module binds the Web Crypto API: the `crypto` global with `getRandomValues`/`randomUUID`, the Promise-typed `SubtleCrypto` interface, a typed `algorithm` params variant, and overloaded `generateKey`/`generateKey_pair`, `importKey`/`importKey_jwk`, `exportKey`/`exportKey_jwk` by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380))
- New `Notification` and `Clipboard` modules bind the Notifications API and the async Clipboard API, including a `notificationAction` class type and `ClipboardItem.supports` by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379))
- `Lwt_js_events.mutation`/`mutations` add Lwt-friendly waiting on `MutationObserver` records, following the existing `click`/`clicks` naming convention, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376), closing [#250](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/250))
- Removed the deprecated `js_of_ocaml-lwt.logger` library and its `lwt_log` dependency since upstream `lwt_log` is itself deprecated, a breaking change for any code still using `Lwt_log_js` by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2378](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2378))
- Fixed wasm-link module naming in `runtime/wasm/args.ml`, which mis-derived module names from `./`-prefixed dune-expanded paths (a change coming in dune 3.24, [ocaml/dune#15156](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15156)) by taking the path basename before stripping `.wat`, by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([#2371](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2371))
- Added `TESTING.md` documenting the in-tree `ppx_expect_light`/`ppx_optcomp_light`/`ppx_light_predicate` inline-test toolchain, including the `[@when]`/`[@@if]` gating mechanisms and how to run a single test by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2375](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2375))
- `manual/api.mld` updated to list `Crypto` on the API reference page alongside the new binding ([072539598](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/072539598387952a1540c2a553ebd877c9581085))
repositories.activity_summary: Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache bindings and an async Web Worker toplevel were opened; ppx_expect_light-based test gating and OCaml 5.5 sync completed.
repositories.activity:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened `Use Wax for runtime files`, a draft reworking how wasm runtime files are built ([#2373](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2373)), and also opened `Wasm runtime clean-up`, a larger reorganization of the wasm runtime sources that merged just after the week closed ([#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2372))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened `Toplevel: Wrapped/Async APIs and a Web Worker toplevel`, adding a `Wrapped` error/warning-capturing API and an `Async` Web Worker toplevel driven over message passing, addressing [#66](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/66) and [#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/833) ([#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2377)), and opened `lib: add Service Worker, MessageChannel and Cache API bindings` covering `ServiceWorker`, `MessageChannel`/`MessagePort`/`MessageEvent` and `Cache`/`CacheStorage`, closing [#1464](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1464) ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381)); both merged shortly after the week ended
- Web API binding coverage expanded with `Crypto` ([#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380)), `Notification`/`Clipboard` ([#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379)), and `MutationObserver` support in `Lwt_js_events` ([#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376)), all merged by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- Test infrastructure was reworked around a new in-tree `ppx_expect_light` that replaces the `ppx_expect` dependency and moves per-profile/version/arch test gating from dune `enabled_if` into source-level `[@when]`/`[@@if]` predicates, closing [#2360](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2360) ([#2369](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2369)), followed by grouping inline-test libraries and dropping the `gen-rules` dune-file generators ([#2370](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2370)), all by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- The CI test matrix moved from OCaml 5.4 to 5.5 and the `tests-ocaml/` suite was resynced with the upstream 5.5 testsuite, importing stdlib coverage for `lib-string`, `lib-hashtbl`, and nine other modules by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374))
- Routine dependency maintenance bumped `actions/checkout` from v4 to v7 in CI workflows via dependabot ([#2368](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2368))
- Ongoing work on initial OCaml 5.6 support by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) continued in the background, merging shortly after this week closed ([#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review of the Service Worker bindings PR surfaced a proposal to consolidate five duplicate messageEvent class types into one.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Review of the Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache bindings ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381)) surfaced a design issue: the library declares five independent, mutually-incompatible `messageEvent` class types across `Dom_html`, `Worker`, `WebSockets`, `EventSource` and `MessageChannel`, even though the DOM spec defines a single shared `MessageEvent` interface; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened a follow-up proposing to consolidate them behind one polymorphic binding with a deprecation path, noting `ExtendableMessageEvent` should stay separate since it extends `ExtendableEvent` instead ([#2382](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2382))
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reviewed the new `Lwt_js_events.mutation`/`mutations` functions and suggested API refinements, questioning whether the observer could be passed to `Lwt.cancel` directly instead of via a ref, and where `mutation` should sit among the other "no plural" event functions ([#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) discussed tooling for editing wasm `.wat` runtime files, noting the difficulty of nested parens, control-flow tracing and poor error reporting, and closed the issue referencing the new `ppx_expect_light`/test-gating work as addressing part of the pain ([#1818](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1818))
repositories.trends_summary: Web API binding coverage keeps expanding while test infrastructure consolidates around a new in-tree ppx_expect_light toolchain.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Systematic expansion of typed bindings for standard browser Web APIs continued, with `Crypto`, `Notification`, `Clipboard` merged this week ([#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380), [#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379)) and `ServiceWorker`/`MessageChannel`/`Cache` opened the same week ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381)), tracked against `API-STATUS.md`
- Test-infrastructure modernization is consolidating scattered dune `enabled_if` gating into a single source-level predicate mechanism (`ppx_expect_light`/`ppx_optcomp_light`), spanning several sequential PRs in one theme ([#2360](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2360), [#2369](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2369), [#2370](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2370), [#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374), [#2375](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2375))
- Forward-looking OCaml version support work is active in parallel, with 5.5 CI/test sync completed ([#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374)) while initial 5.6 support continues in a long-running PR ([#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227))
- Several PR descriptions this week note being "Generated with Claude Code," reflecting growing use of AI-assisted tooling for binding generation and large refactors ([#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2377), [#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381), [#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379), [#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380))
repositories.contributors_summary: hhugo led Web API bindings and test-infrastructure work; vouillon, shonfeder and balat contributed wasm fixes, runtime work and review feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged bindings for `Crypto` ([#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380)) and `Notification`/`Clipboard` ([#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379)), added `MutationObserver` support to `Lwt_js_events` closing [#250](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/250) ([#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376)), removed the deprecated `lwt_log` logger ([#2378](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2378)), reworked the test infrastructure around `ppx_expect_light` ([#2369](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2369), [#2370](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2370)), resynced the test suite with OCaml 5.5 ([#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374)), wrote `TESTING.md` ([#2375](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2375)), opened the toplevel Wrapped/Async work ([#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2377)) and the Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache bindings ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381)), and closed several issues including [#1788](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1788), [#1818](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1818), [#1929](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1929) and [#2360](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2360), and opened the `messageEvent` consolidation proposal ([#2382](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2382))
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened the wasm runtime clean-up ([#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2372)) and the draft `Use Wax for runtime files` PR ([#2373](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2373)), continuing work on initial OCaml 5.6 support ([#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227))
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) fixed wasm-link module-name derivation in `runtime/wasm/args.ml` to keep compatibility with dune 3.24's path representation ([#2371](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2371))
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reviewed the `MutationObserver` Lwt integration with API feedback on cancellation and function placement ([#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376))
- Dependabot opened the routine `actions/checkout` v4-to-v7 upgrade, merged this week ([#2368](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2368))

repositories.brief_summary: Runtime 4 was retired, an `-experimental-optimizations` flag landed, and AVX512 backend work opened alongside a new compiler-performance CI.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `-experimental-optimizations` flag, `ocamlfilt` demangler tool, `Punspecializedarray` array kind, and runtime-4 removal.
repositories.new_features:
- New `-experimental-optimizations` compiler flag bundles a set of codegen optimizations (CFG prologue shrink-wrap/validate, x86 peephole, `SPLIT_AROUND_LOOPS`/`AFFINITY` regalloc params, `irc` allocator, CFG block merging) that are off by default, added by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([#6174](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6174)).
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) removed the OCaml-4 runtime from the tree along with associated dual-runtime technical debt, retiring the OCaml 4/5 runtime switch and updating `README.md` accordingly ([#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) landed `ocamlfilt`, a pure-OCaml demangler for compiler symbol names that auto-detects the pre-5.3 flat, post-5.3 flat, and new structured mangling schemes, reading from stdin or the command line ([#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)).
- [Jérémie Dimino](https://github.com/jdimino-js) added an explicit `Punspecializedarray` array kind, replacing an overloaded use of `Pgenarray` that misbehaved when the flat float array optimization is disabled ([#6101](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6101)).
- [@zhizhuoxin](https://github.com/zhizhuoxin) introduced a new `Allocation` mode axis to improve static support for the `zero_alloc` checker, printing basic modes for function arguments and return values ([#6269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6269)).
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) enabled implicit kind declarations inside structures (previously restricted to signatures) and documented the change ([#6114](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6114)).
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) allowed zero-parameter, unused-parameter, and multi-parameter custom `[@@or_null]` payload shapes, inferring the result kind from the unary payload type ([#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5830)).
- [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) fixed the representation of inline records with `any`-kind fields, which previously caused variant match arms to be selected incorrectly ([#6240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6240)).
- [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) flipped the default value of `cfg_eliminate_dead_trap_handlers` to on ([#6267](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6267)) and refactored CFG printing ([#6117](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6117)).
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) fixed a bad `movz` instruction emitted for arm64 stack reallocation ([#6311](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6311)) and added bounds checks to arm64 AST constructors ([#6313](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6313)).
- [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) fixed a layout bug affecting aliases in pattern matching ([#6152](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6152)) and guarded data placed in the text section with `ud2` instructions ([#6129](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6129)).
- [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) made an empty `let poly_` binding a hard error rather than a warning ([#6195](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6195)) and added support for `val poly_` ([#6287](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6287), tracking [#5755](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5755)).
- [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) fixed double printing in `ocamlnat -dparsetree`/`-dsource` ([#6166](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6166)).
- [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) had `eval.mli` copied into the compiler install directory so it is available to downstream tooling ([#6207](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6207)).
- Documentation was updated to reflect the OCaml-4 runtime removal in `README.md` ([#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)), to note that implicit kind declarations now work in structures in `jane/doc/extensions/_06-kinds/05-implicit.md` ([#6114](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6114)), and to correct the SIMD intro guide's float-array example to use `Float_array.of_array`/`Floatarray.get` ([#6128](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6128)).
repositories.activity_summary: AVX512 backend work, name-mangling and DWARF debugging cleanups, and a run of runtime concurrency fixes opened this week.
repositories.activity:
- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) opened a multi-PR **AVX512 backend** effort completing 512-bit vector/mask support, including mask-layout plumbing ([#6293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6293)), instruction encoding ([#6294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6294)), core backend support with a `Const_mask` cmm constructor and ported SIMD tests ([#6339](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6339)), and AVX512 array operations ([#6340](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6340)); the same author also opened work on fibers holding a table of dynamic-binding stacks ([#6359](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6359)).
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened a series of runtime concurrency fixes covering a bytecode `RESUME` stack-corruption bug ([#6332](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6332)), a signal-handler installation race ([#6342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6342)), non-atomic bigarray proxy creation that could leak proxies ([#6344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6344)), and a lock-free skiplist race ([#6346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6346)), and also opened a `make` fix so it notices runtime changes ([#6325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6325)).
- [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened a run of CFG backend cleanups including a shared dead-code predicate ([#6301](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6301)), a documented invariant in `Cfg_comballoc` ([#6302](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6302)), `Cfg_loop_infos` improvements ([#6315](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6315)), CR-soons in `Cfg_dataflow` ([#6324](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6324)), reducibility checks in `Cfg_prologue.run` ([#6328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6328)), and a new `[%%expect_asm_full]` codegen-test attribute paired with a stack-check/SIMD bug fix ([#6334](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6334)).
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) opened further **structured name-mangling** work covering binding operators and effect handlers ([#6292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6292)), a `Lazy` path item ([#6303](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6303)), and functor instances ([#6362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6362)), following the merged `ocamlfilt` demangler ([#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)).
- [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened DWARF/debugging tweaks and cleanup items ([#6351](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6351), [#6354](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6354), [#6355](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6355)).
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a **compiler-performance CI** workflow that builds base and head compilers and benchmarks compile time on a corpus of real compiler source files ([#6365](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6365)), with calibration PRs introducing a synthetic 2-3% slowdown ([#6364](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6364)), a no-op control PR ([#6363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6363)), and a test run against [#6113](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6113) ([#6358](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6358)).
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened work integrating the Merlin **typing-recovery mechanism** directly into the type checker, aiming to reduce divergence between Merlin's and the compiler's type checkers ([#6366](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6366)).
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) had a PR on instance type constructors in `typeopt` merged ([#6113](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6113)) and then reverted by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) the same week ([#6343](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6343)), and separately opened follow-on work on instance type constructor arguments ([#6352](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6352)) and a WIP refinement-syntax draft for LLM-integration testing ([#6335](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6335)).
- [@zhizhuoxin](https://github.com/zhizhuoxin) followed up the new `Allocation` mode axis ([#6269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6269)) by opening heap-allocation detection to constrain that mode, distinguishing `stack_`-marked allocations from others ([#6319](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6319)).
- [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) merged interior-pointer access primitives `%unsafe_ptr_{get,set}` for bytecode ([#6295](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6295)) and adding `int64_u`/`int32_u`/`nativeint_u` to predef ([#6321](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6321)), and opened a fix for unsound argument value kinds in GADT matches ([#6356](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6356)).
- [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) merged a poison-value addition to Flambda 2 terms ([#6273](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6273)) and counting removed operations during inlining ([#5991](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5991)), and opened a fix for continuations called with invalid kinds ([#6291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6291)).
- [Pierre Chambart](https://github.com/chambart) opened work following relation variables and function parameters in Flambda 2 ([#6317](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6317)) and compact printing for `-dflambda` ([#6338](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6338)).
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) merged replacing several `Map.find` calls with `Map.find_or_null` in Flambda 2 for compilation speed ([#6290](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6290)) and opened a fix so Flambda 2 does not silently forget about bottom ([#6347](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6347)).
- [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury) opened a continuation specialization budget for Flambda 2's match-in-match handling ([#6305](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6305)).
- [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) opened recording of unyielding calls in bytecode debug events, tracking whether a function application can perform a free effect ([#6251](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6251)).
- [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) worked through review feedback from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) on functional mode polymorphism, integrating mode-relation operations into type inference behind an `-extension mode_polymorphism_alpha` flag ([#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483)), and merged a fix to `Const.comonadic_to_monadic_min` ([#6244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6244)).
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened inclusion of Merlin in the compiler install ([#6288](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6288)).
- [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) opened a fix for runtime heap-to-stack pointers in `Translquote` ([#6316](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6316)).
repositories.discussions_summary: Soundness bugs in the zero_alloc checker and a proposal to rework `[@inline]` attribute semantics prompted design discussion.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) reported that `[@zero_alloc assume never_returns_normally]` is accepted by the checker on code that actually allocates when an inlined function raises an exception, and further noted in the same thread that plain `[@zero_alloc assume]` has the same unsoundness ([#6297](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6297)); a related report showed the checker can also accept code ending in an allocating `raise_notrace` under `-Oclassic` ([#6261](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6261)).
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened a design discussion proposing to rework `[@inline]` attribute semantics, arguing the current meaning conflates cross-compilation-unit availability with automatic-inlining heuristics, especially in combination with `[@inlined]` ([#6312](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6312)).
- On functional mode polymorphism, [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) flagged repeated instances of `with_zap_scope` resolving the zap scope before the traversal runs, prompting a series of fixes across `close_type`, `closed_extension_constructor`, `closed_class`, and `closed_type_decl` call sites ([#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483)).
- On scannable axes for abstract kinds, [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) discussed whether abstract-kind substitution could leave redundant internal representations like `Sort(float64, non_pointer)`, and whether the equivalence/subkinding relation should be documented declaratively ([#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874)).
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) questioned [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)'s approach to recording unyielding calls in bytecode debug events, suggesting comonadic modes be joined before projecting the yielding axis so the logic can be reused for other axes like purity ([#6251](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6251)).
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) reviewed [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes)'s runtime-4 retirement commit-by-commit, questioning whether some mechanisms (e.g. `ocamlc -config` parsing behavior) should be preserved for diff-reduction with upstream ([#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
repositories.trends_summary: AI-assisted ("100% vibe") backend/debugging cleanups and runtime concurrency hardening were prominent this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A large share of backend and debugging cleanup PRs this week carry a `100% vibe` label, spanning [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)'s CFG refactors ([#6117](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6117), [#6299](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6299), [#6301](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6301), [#6302](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6302), [#6306](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6306), [#6315](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6315), [#6324](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6324), [#6328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6328), [#6334](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6334)), [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)'s DWARF/debugging fixes ([#6129](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6129), [#6152](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6152), [#6351](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6351), [#6354](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6354), [#6355](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6355)), and [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)'s arm64 correctness fixes ([#6311](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6311), [#6313](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6313)), suggesting increasing use of AI-assisted patches for mechanical backend hardening.
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened multiple runtime concurrency/race fixes in one week ([#6332](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6332), [#6342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6342), [#6344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6344), [#6346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6346)), several found and drafted with AI assistance per the PR descriptions, following on from the runtime-4 retirement that simplified the runtime codebase ([#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
- Investment in developer tooling continued with the new `ocamlfilt` demangler ([#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)) and its structured-mangling follow-ups ([#6292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6292), [#6303](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6303), [#6362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6362)), alongside a new compiler-performance CI to catch regressions such as a synthetic 2-3% slowdown ([#6363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6363), [#6364](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6364), [#6365](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6365)).
- AVX512 backend support progressed as a multi-PR series from [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#6293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6293), [#6294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6294), [#6339](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6339), [#6340](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6340)), following the same incremental, split-PR pattern used for the `Allocation` mode axis by [@zhizhuoxin](https://github.com/zhizhuoxin) ([#6269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6269), [#6319](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6319)).
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributors spanned AVX512 backend work, runtime concurrency fixes, Flambda 2 work, name-mangling tooling, and typing/mode discussions.

repositories.brief_summary: Vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings merged; RWF flags PR and vendored-liburing discussion remain open.
repositories.new_features_summary: Added vectored `readv_fixed`/`writev_fixed` bindings for fixed buffers and fixed misnamed C stubs ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)).
repositories.new_features:
- Added `readv_fixed` and `writev_fixed` to the `Uring` API, letting callers submit multi-buffer reads and writes against a ring's registered **fixed buffer** in a single operation, analogous to the existing `read_fixed`/`write_fixed` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Added validation that raises `Invalid_argument` if any buffer passed to `readv_fixed`/`writev_fixed` does not belong to the ring's fixed buffer, guarding against misuse before submission ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Fixed a naming bug where the existing `read_fixed`/`write_fixed` functions called into C stubs named `submit_readv_fixed`/`submit_writev_fixed` without ever performing vectored I/O; renamed to `submit_read_fixed`/`submit_write_fixed` so the vectored names became available for the genuinely vectored operations ([56699618](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/commit/56699618217cb588a1045ac43d1727e312544998))
- Extended `tests/main.md` with examples exercising the new vectored fixed-buffer operations ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
repositories.activity_summary: PR #155 merged for vectored fixed-buffer bindings; RWF flags PR #154 and vendored-liburing issue #156 remain active.
repositories.activity:
- The **vectored fixed-buffer bindings** work was completed and merged by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), correcting the misnamed stubs along the way, with review approval from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- The **per-operation RWF flags** PR by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), which exposes `preadv2`/`pwritev2`-style flags such as `dsync` and `nocache` at the uring layer for later use in Eio_linux, remains open; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) asked for a rebase now that the fixed-buffer bindings landed, and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) reviewed the changes favorably ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154))
- Discussion continues on whether the package should support building against **system `liburing`** instead of only the vendored copy, following a build failure on Debian caused by a missing `io_uring_prep_readv_fixed` declaration ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate over supporting system liburing instead of the vendored copy, prompted by a Debian build failure ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) discussed whether to support building against system `liburing` rather than the vendored copy; Debian's patched build fails with an implicit-declaration error for `io_uring_prep_readv_fixed`, while opam-installed `uring` often needs a newer `liburing` than stable distributions ship, illustrating the tension between packaging stability and access to newer kernel features ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
repositories.trends_summary: Fixed-buffer/vectored I/O API expansion continues alongside packaging concerns about liburing versioning ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154), [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155), [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Continued work around **fixed/registered buffer operations** — first correcting stub naming and then adding vectored variants — points to an ongoing effort to broaden zero-copy I/O primitive coverage ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154), [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Packaging and distribution concerns around **vendored vs system `liburing`** are surfacing as the library adopts kernel-version-dependent features, since newer operations require `liburing` versions ahead of what stable distributions package ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
repositories.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy merged the vectored fixed-buffer bindings and continued the RWF flags PR, with review input from Thomas Leonard and Patrick Ferris, and packaging discussion from Stéphane Glondu.
repositories.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) implemented and merged the vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings, including the C stub renaming fix ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)), and continued work on the per-operation RWF flags PR that remains open ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed and approved the vectored fixed-buffer bindings ([#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)), requested a rebase of the RWF flags PR after that merge ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154)), and discussed the vendored-vs-system `liburing` build concerns raised in the issue tracker ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) reviewed the per-operation RWF flags changes and confirmed they looked useful ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154))
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) raised and discussed the request to build without vendored `liburing`, sharing Debian packaging patches used to work around the current vendoring approach ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))

repositories.brief_summary: Portable and Linux-specific socket-option APIs landed in Eio.Net.Sockopt, and Eio.Process.pipe's return types were relaxed for better interop.
repositories.new_features_summary: New Eio.Net.Sockopt API for portable and Linux-specific socket options, plus relaxed Eio.Process.pipe return types.
repositories.new_features:
- Added an extensible **`Eio.Net.Sockopt`** type together with `Eio.Net.setsockopt`/`getsockopt`, a `register_printer` mechanism (so `Sockopt.pp`/`pp_binding` work like OCaml's exception printers), and a new `Invalid_option` error case, laying the groundwork for typed socket options across all backends by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), based on [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s original design ([#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575))
- Added portable option constructors — `SO_DEBUG`, `SO_BROADCAST`, `SO_REUSEADDR`, `SO_KEEPALIVE`, `SO_DONTROUTE`, `SO_OOBINLINE`, `TCP_NODELAY`, `IPV6_ONLY`, `SO_REUSEPORT`, `SO_SNDBUF`, `SO_RCVBUF`, `SO_RCVLOWAT`, `SO_SNDLOWAT`, `SO_LINGER`, `SO_RCVTIMEO`, `SO_SNDTIMEO` — usable even outside Unix processes (e.g. mocks, unikernels), by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), closing [#322](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/322) and [#376](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/376) ([#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859))
- Added **Linux-specific `TCP_*`/`IP_*` socket options** (`TCP_CORK`, `TCP_KEEPIDLE`, `TCP_KEEPINTVL`, `TCP_KEEPCNT`, `TCP_USER_TIMEOUT`, `TCP_MAXSEG`, `TCP_LINGER2`, `TCP_DEFER_ACCEPT`, `TCP_CONGESTION`, `TCP_SYNCNT`, `TCP_WINDOW_CLAMP`, `TCP_QUICKACK`, `TCP_FASTOPEN`, `IP_FREEBIND`, `IP_BIND_ADDRESS_NO_PORT`, `IP_LOCAL_PORT_RANGE`, `IP_TTL`, `IP_MTU`, `IP_MTU_DISCOVER`) implemented via `getsockopt`/`setsockopt` C stubs in `lib_eio_linux`, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575))
- Relaxed the return type of **`Eio.Process.pipe`** from a fixed closable-source/sink pair to a polymorphic-variant subtype, allowing the resulting flows to be used directly where monomorphic types are expected (e.g. `Cohttp_eio.Body.t`), by [Isaac Arogbonlo](https://github.com/Arogbonlo) with casts added by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), closing [#750](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/750) ([#775](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/775))
repositories.activity_summary: A follow-on SO_TYPE query PR was opened while a stale Async-integration draft was closed and pty/login_tty work continued.
repositories.activity:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened **`add SO_TYPE query to the portable Eio socket interface`**, a follow-on to the `Sockopt` design discussion, exploring whether querying a socket's type belongs in the portable API ([#860](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/860))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) closed the long-stale **`eio_linux: add support for integration with Async`** draft, citing lack of continued interest ([#221](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/221))
- The **pseudoterminal (`Eio_unix.Pty`) and `login_tty` fork-action** work by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) continued towards completion, with a FreeBSD-specific test failure (pty buffers discarded on close, unlike Linux) diagnosed with shell access provided by [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers), plus design discussion with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on re-exporting `Unix.tcgetattr` and why `logintty(3)` from libc was avoided in favor of direct syscalls ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- Review of the **`Sockopt` API surface** included discussion of whether to expose `SO_TYPE` as a raw integer, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) agreeing to drop it from the portable option list until it can be mapped to a proper variant type rather than an unhelpful platform-dependent integer ([#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859))
- Issues **`Relax the return types of Eio.Process.pipe`** ([#750](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/750), reported by [Rizo](https://github.com/rizo)), **`Support for socket options`** ([#322](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/322), reported by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)), and **`Support retrieving socket options`** ([#376](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/376), reported by [Bikal Lem](https://github.com/bikallem)) were all closed this week by their corresponding implementation PRs
repositories.trends_summary: Work concentrated on filling gaps between Eio's typed API and the underlying Unix socket/process interfaces.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Continued effort to give Eio **typed, portable equivalents of raw Unix APIs** — socket options via `Eio.Net.Sockopt` ([#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859), [#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575), [#860](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/860)) and pseudoterminal/session handling via `Eio_unix.Pty` and `login_tty` ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531)) rather than requiring users to drop down to `Unix` directly
- Large single-author PRs are being **split and restructured during review** to ease landing — [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) divided [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s original `Sockopt` PR ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575)) into a base API PR ([#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858)) and a portable-options PR ([#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859))
- Preference for **relaxing overly restrictive polymorphic variant types** (as in [#775](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/775)'s `Eio.Process.pipe` change) over asking downstream libraries to widen their own type signatures
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors were active on socket-option APIs, process-pipe typing, pty support, and cross-platform test debugging.
repositories.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) restructured and merged the socket-options work into base and portable-options PRs ([#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859)), added casts and force-pushed a fix to land [Isaac Arogbonlo](https://github.com/Arogbonlo)'s `Process.pipe` change ([#775](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/775)), closed the stale Async-integration draft ([#221](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/221)), and reviewed the pty/`login_tty` PR ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) authored the original Linux socket-options PR that the week's work was based on ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575)), opened a follow-on PR exploring `SO_TYPE` support ([#860](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/860)), participated in API design discussion around error handling for truncated buffers, and continued developing pseudoterminal/`login_tty` support with FreeBSD-specific fixes ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- [Isaac Arogbonlo](https://github.com/Arogbonlo) implemented the relaxed return types for `Eio.Process.pipe`, resolving a type-compatibility problem with libraries like `Cohttp_eio` ([#775](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/775))
- [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) provided FreeBSD shell access that helped diagnose a pty test failure caused by platform differences in buffer handling on close ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- [Rizo](https://github.com/rizo), [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), and [Bikal Lem](https://github.com/bikallem) originally reported the issues resolved this week concerning `Process.pipe` typing and socket-option support ([#750](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/750), [#322](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/322), [#376](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/376))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Opam-repository shipped new packages plus dune 3.24.0 and OCaml 5.5.0 support, while ocaml.org hardened its watch.ocaml.org scraper.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Opam-repository added 4+ new packages, dune 3.24.0, OCaml 5.5.0 manual/tooling, and melange 7.0.0; ocaml.org made scraping fault-tolerant.
group_summaries.new_features:
**ocaml/opam-repository**
- New packages: **soteria** by [opale](https://github.com/N1ark) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30150](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30150)), **playwright** OCaml bindings by [Dmytro Melnychuk](https://github.com/dmytro-melno) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30146](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30146)), **osx-keychain** by [Alex Leighton](https://github.com/alexleighton) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30148](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30148)), and **forcamla** by [Christopher Sumnicht](https://github.com/thyrgle), iterated from 0.1.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30124](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30124), closed) to 0.2.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30142](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30142))

- **dune 3.24.0** released across 18 packages by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092))

**ocaml/ocaml.org**
- The `watch.ocaml.org` **video scraper** now catches per-source fetch failures (network outage, expired TLS cert, malformed response) as a non-fatal `Scrape_report.Error` instead of aborting the whole Planet scrape job, by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695))
- Bumped `actions/checkout` from v6 to v7 across all CI workflows ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3691))
- Added the platform newsletter covering February-May 2026, by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3680))
- Automated Planet blog aggregation and platform-release scrapes pulled in new posts and release data ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3677](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3677), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3692), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3697](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3697))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) had a high-volume week: four new packages, **dune 3.24.0** across 18 packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)), the **OCaml 5.5.0** manual with matching merlin/ocaml-lsp-server support ([ocaml/opam-repository#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095), [ocaml/opam-repository#30117](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30117)), and **melange 7.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30094](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30094))
- The dune 3.24.0 release forced immediate downstream upper-bound pins ([ocaml/opam-repository#30143](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30143), [ocaml/opam-repository#30132](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30132)), and several packages needed source tarballs recovered into opam-source-archives after upstream links broke, led by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri)
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) had lighter, incident-driven activity: [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) fixed and closed out a scraper outage ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3694)) and a site-downtime report prompted an uptime-monitoring question ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3699](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3699))
- Five ocaml.org content PRs remain open awaiting merge, covering AWS industrial users, a sqlite3 FTS5 cookbook example, and a GC tutorial clarification ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3681), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3698](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3698), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3682))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Opam-repository saw high release volume and dune-3.24 pin fallout; ocaml.org advanced AWS, GC-tutorial, and sqlite3 cookbook PRs.
