group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- No PRs directly referenced each other across the two repositories this week, but [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)'s automated platform-release scrape ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693)) structurally depends on package releases published in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository), such as this week's dune 3.24.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) and melange 7.0.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30094](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30094))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) was active in both repositories: discussing source-archive policy in opam-repository's wol checksum issue ([ocaml/opam-repository#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089)), while ocaml.org's automated Planet scrape aggregated his blog posts ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3677](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3677))
- The OCaml 5.5.0 ecosystem readiness work in opam-repository (manual, merlin, ocaml-lsp-server, [ocaml/opam-repository#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095), [ocaml/opam-repository#30117](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30117)) is the kind of platform release that ocaml.org's release-tracking pages surface to users, though no PR on ocaml.org referenced it directly this week
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/opam-repository#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089): a bad checksum on the **wol** packages was traced to a change in how the tangled.org forge generates git-archive tarballs; [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported it, [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) and [akshay](https://github.com/oppiliappan) diagnosed the cause, [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) cited repository policy against modifying source-archive checksums, and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) discussed publishing a static, annotated-tag archive instead
- [ocaml/opam-repository#30121](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30121): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) discussed whether OCaml "trunk" compiler-variant packages that intentionally omit checksums should be archived, concluding most are git-branch sources that don't need it
- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3699](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3699): [Rafał](https://github.com/rafrafek) reported ocaml.org was down and asked whether the project uses an uptime monitor, suggesting StatusCake or Pulsetic
- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3694): [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) traced the Planet scrape job failure to an expired `watch.ocaml.org` TLS certificate, noting the blog scraper already isolated per-source failures while the video scraper did not
- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3682): [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) and [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) discussed preserving Anil Madhavapeddy's and Yaron Minsky's original Real World OCaml GC tutorial text while adding a preamble and appendix for `Stdlib` equivalents to `Core`
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Dune version-bound fallout**: the 3.24.0 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) required immediate upper-bound pins on cppo, lablgtk3, wasm_of_ocaml-compiler ([ocaml/opam-repository#30143](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30143)) and camomile ([ocaml/opam-repository#30132](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30132)) to keep existing packages installable
- **Recurring archive breakage**: several packages lost upstream source tarballs to 404s or checksum drift this week ([ocaml/opam-repository#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089), [ocaml/opam-repository#30104](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30104), [ocaml/opam-repository#30110](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30110), [ocaml/opam-repository#30122](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30122)), each resolved by re-hosting in opam-source-archives
- **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem readiness**: the manual, merlin and ocaml-lsp-server all shipped 5.5 support in the same week ([ocaml/opam-repository#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095), [ocaml/opam-repository#30117](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30117))
- **Per-source error isolation** is spreading across ocaml.org's scraper codebase after an outage exposed a gap in `watch.ocaml.org` handling, with further hardening already tracked ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3696](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3696))
- **Community-contributed content** keeps arriving on ocaml.org from outside the core team: a sqlite3 FTS5 example ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3698](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3698)) and an AWS industrial-users entry ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3681))

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5 shipped in merlin, ocaml-lsp and js_of_ocaml, while dune advanced RPC refactoring and opam progressed its tar-based repo rewrite.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune shipped shell completion, three repos gained OCaml 5.5 support, opam added native tar handling, and js_of_ocaml shipped new Web API bindings.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** added command/subcommand/flag completion for bash, zsh, and pwsh via `dune completion [shell]`, by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) ([ocaml/dune#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14779)); `dune build --auto-promote` and `dune fmt` now exit 0 on success, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#15284](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15284)); `blang` boolean expressions in `enabled_if` are now lazily short-circuited, by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) ([ocaml/dune#15291](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15291))
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)** merged **OCaml 5.5 support** and published release **v5.8-505**, by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077), [ocaml/merlin#2083](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2083)), bundling a signature-help fix for type aliases ([ocaml/merlin#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067)) and a locate fix for punned let bindings ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066))
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)** published release **1.27.0**, adding **OCaml 5.5** support ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609)) and no longer forcing Merlin's `real_paths = false`, letting full (non-shortened) type-alias paths show in errors, fixed by [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579), closes [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1395))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)** added a native `OpamTar` module that reads/writes tar.gz archives via the `tar` library instead of shelling out, by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) building on work by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945)); added a conditional hard-upgrade path for the opam root format ([ocaml/opam#6949](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6949)); and opam file parse errors now print the originating repository ([ocaml/opam#6971](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6971))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)** merged new `Crypto` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380)) and `Notification`/`Clipboard` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379)) Web API bindings, and `Lwt_js_events.mutation`/`mutations` for `MutationObserver` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376), closing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#250](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/250)), all by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo); removed the deprecated `js_of_ocaml-lwt.logger`/`lwt_log` library, a breaking change ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2378](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2378))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** shipped shell completion and a zero-exit `--auto-promote`, ran an extended RPC/watch-mode refactor, and closed dozens of long-standing issues
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)** released **v5.8-505** with OCaml 5.5 support and continued reviewing an indexer memory-usage PR
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)** published release **1.27.0**, bundling the OCaml 5.5 upgrade and a long-open `-short-paths` Merlin-configuration fix
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)** advanced its tar-based repository-format rewrite ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6625)) and merged several portability and CI fixes
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)** opened an investigation into `odoc_driver` module resolution for packages with sibling libraries sharing module names (Eliom's client/server split, [ocaml/odoc#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450))
- **[ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)** continued discussing a fix for a stublibs warning triggered under OCaml 5.5 ([ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)** merged new Crypto, Notification and Clipboard Web API bindings and reworked its test infrastructure around a new in-tree `ppx_expect_light` toolchain
- **ocaml/opam-file-format** and **ocaml-dune/spawn** had no recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune's RPC subsystem and nix flake saw major refactors, opam's tar rewrite progressed via several merged PRs, and js_of_ocaml opened Service Worker bindings.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) drove an extended **RPC/watch-mode refactor**, consolidating client lifecycle, cancellation, and long-polling into the core build system ([ocaml/dune#15345](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15345), [ocaml/dune#15346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15346), [ocaml/dune#15330](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15330), [ocaml/dune#15362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15362), [ocaml/dune#15363](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15363), [ocaml/dune#15328](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15328), [ocaml/dune#15329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15329)); [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) split the monolithic `flake.nix` into per-purpose modules for Rocq, OxCaml and the dune package ([ocaml/dune#15318](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15318), [ocaml/dune#15312](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15312), [ocaml/dune#15302](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15302))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: the repository-format rewrite tracked in [ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6625) progressed with the `ocaml-tar` 3.5.0 bump ([ocaml/opam#6976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6976)) and reftest fixups ([ocaml/opam#6970](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6970)); new PRs opened for local-repository symlinks by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([ocaml/opam#6977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6977)) and cross-user git repositories by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([ocaml/opam#6980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6980), fixes [ocaml/opam#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963))
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) continued iterating on an **LRU cache for indexing memory usage** in response to review by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([ocaml/merlin#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079), building on [ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)), and closed an ocamlgrep structural-search integration proposal from [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache bindings ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381), closing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1464](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1464)) and a Wrapped/Async Web Worker toplevel ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2377)); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened a wasm runtime clean-up ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2372)) and continued OCaml 5.6 groundwork ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227))
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) began reproducing the sibling-library module-resolution report by building Eliom's docs with odoc_driver ([ocaml/odoc#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450))
- **[ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)**: [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) proposed a patch normalizing `ld.conf` paths to absolute form in `read_ldconf`, discussing reliability tradeoffs with `Fl_split.norm_dir` versus `Unix.stat` inode comparison ([ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **OCaml 5.5 rollout**: support merged in lockstep across [ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077) (released as v5.8-505) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609) (released as 1.27.0), with [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) resyncing its CI/test matrix to 5.5 the same week ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374)); [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)'s open stublibs-warning issue is also specific to OCaml 5.5 ([ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115))
- **dune → js_of_ocaml**: a wasm-link module-name fix in js_of_ocaml was made in direct anticipation of an upcoming dune 3.24 change to how paths are expanded, cross-referencing [ocaml/dune#15156](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15156) from [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2371](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2371), fixed by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- **dune ↔ odoc**: dune sandboxed its odoc documentation-generation rules ([ocaml/dune#15190](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15190)) and separately opened a fix for odoc's `@doc-new` fallback on build-directory `cmti` paths, by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([ocaml/dune#15293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15293))
- **dune ↔ merlin ↔ ocaml-lsp**: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) traced a dune-vs-ocaml-lsp ppx error discrepancy to Dune running ppxes from `_build/default` while Merlin starts them from the source-tree root, moving the underlying report into merlin for investigation ([ocaml/merlin#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082), duplicate closed at [ocaml/merlin#1965](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1965))
- **Coordinated dependency maintenance**: a Dependabot `actions/checkout` v6→v7 bump landed the same week across [ocaml/merlin#2081](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2081), [ocaml/odoc#1451](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1451), [ocaml/opam#6969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6969), and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2368](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2368)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **[ocaml/dune#15340](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15340)**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reported dune 3.23.1 failing to build on the cygwin backend, closed the same week alongside a blake3/`foreign_library` Cygwin fix ([ocaml/dune#15343](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15343))
- **[ocaml/dune#15290](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15290)**: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reported an internal error with `dune build --pkg=enable @doc-new` on a locked META-only library, reproduced in a test by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([ocaml/dune#15292](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15292))
- **[ocaml/merlin#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082)**: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) identified the likely root cause of the dune-vs-ocaml-lsp ppx error discrepancy in mismatched working directories for ppx execution
- **[ocaml/merlin#2080](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2080)**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) debated why merlin.el truncates multi-line error messages, and whether the fix belongs in `merlin.el` or `ocaml-eglot`
- **[ocaml/odoc#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450)**: [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) described how odoc_driver resolves same-named sibling-library modules to the wrong library for Eliom's `eliom.client`/`eliom.server` split; [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) confirmed reproduction and is investigating
- **[ocaml/opam#6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966)**: [lexa](https://github.com/qexat) questioned why opam's solver proposed downgrading `ansifmt` from 2.0.0 to 0.3.0 as part of a `re` upgrade
- **[ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115)**: [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) confirmed reproducing the ocamlfind stublibs warning under OCaml 5.5, and [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) proposed a candidate fix
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2382](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2382)**: review of the Service Worker bindings surfaced that the library declares five independent, mutually incompatible `messageEvent` class types despite a single shared DOM `MessageEvent` interface; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) proposed consolidating them
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Shell-outs replaced by native OCaml**: [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)'s tar rewrite ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945), [ocaml/opam#6976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6976) feeding [ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6625)) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)'s growing native Web API binding surface ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381))
- **OCaml 5.5 as a cross-cutting rollout theme**, landing in merlin, ocaml-lsp and js_of_ocaml's test suite within the same week, with ocamlfind's open bug also tied to the same release
- **Continued consolidation of dune's RPC/watch-mode logic** into the core build system rather than separate wrappers ([ocaml/dune#15328](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15328), [ocaml/dune#15329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15329), [ocaml/dune#15333](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15333), [ocaml/dune#15348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15348), [ocaml/dune#15362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15362), [ocaml/dune#15363](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15363))
- **Growing investment in test infrastructure**: opam added a unit-test framework for `OpamFilename` and exhaustive reftests ([ocaml/opam#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6953), [ocaml/opam#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6956)) while js_of_ocaml consolidated dune `enabled_if` gating into a new `ppx_expect_light` toolchain ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2369](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2369), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2370](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2370), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374))
- **AI-assisted PR generation** noted explicitly in several js_of_ocaml PR descriptions ("Generated with Claude Code") for binding and refactor work ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2377), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381))

group_summaries.brief_summary: oxcaml/oxcaml retired the OCaml-4 runtime, added an -experimental-optimizations flag, and opened AVX512 backend and runtime concurrency work.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New -experimental-optimizations flag, ocamlfilt demangler, Punspecializedarray array kind, and OCaml-4 runtime removal landed in oxcaml/oxcaml.
group_summaries.new_features:
- New `-experimental-optimizations` compiler flag bundling off-by-default codegen improvements (CFG prologue shrink-wrap, x86 peephole, `irc` allocator, CFG block merging), added by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6174](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6174).
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) removed the OCaml-4 runtime and the dual-runtime switch from oxcaml/oxcaml, retiring the associated technical debt ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) landed `ocamlfilt`, a demangler for compiler symbol names that auto-detects pre-5.3, post-5.3, and new structured mangling schemes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)).
- [Jérémie Dimino](https://github.com/jdimino-js) added an explicit `Punspecializedarray` array kind, replacing an overloaded use of `Pgenarray` that misbehaved with the flat float array optimization disabled ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6101](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6101)).
- [@zhizhuoxin](https://github.com/zhizhuoxin) introduced a new `Allocation` mode axis to improve static support for the `zero_alloc` checker ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6269)).
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) enabled implicit kind declarations inside structures, previously restricted to signatures ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6114](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6114)).
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) allowed zero-parameter, unused-parameter, and multi-parameter custom `[@@or_null]` payload shapes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5830)).
- [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) fixed inline-record representation for `any`-kind fields that had caused incorrect variant match-arm selection ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6240)).
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) fixed a bad `movz` instruction emitted for arm64 stack reallocation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6311](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6311)) and added bounds checks to arm64 AST constructors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6313](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6313)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- oxcaml/oxcaml was the only repository in the group with recorded activity in week 26; [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository), [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin), [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml), and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no tracked commits or pull requests this week.
- Two cleanups of note landed in oxcaml/oxcaml: removal of the legacy OCaml-4 runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)) and a new `-experimental-optimizations` flag bundling several off-by-default codegen improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6174](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6174)).
- New backend work opened this week includes a multi-PR AVX512 vector/mask support effort from [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6293), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6294), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6339](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6339), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6340](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6340)) and a new compiler-performance CI from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6365](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6365)).
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened a cluster of runtime concurrency/race fixes covering bytecode `RESUME`, signal handling, bigarray proxies, and the lock-free skiplist ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6332](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6332), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6342), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6344), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6346)).
- Two soundness issues were reported in the `zero_alloc` checker, relating to `[@zero_alloc assume never_returns_normally]` and allocating `raise_notrace` under `-Oclassic` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6297](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6297), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6261](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6261)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: AVX512 backend work, structured name-mangling, and a run of runtime concurrency and CFG backend fixes opened in oxcaml/oxcaml.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) opened a multi-PR AVX512 backend effort: mask-layout plumbing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6293)), instruction encoding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6294)), core backend support with a `Const_mask` cmm constructor ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6339](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6339)), and AVX512 array operations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6340](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6340)); the same author opened fiber dynamic-binding-stack tracking ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6359](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6359)).
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened runtime concurrency fixes for a bytecode `RESUME` stack-corruption bug ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6332](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6332)), a signal-handler installation race ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6342)), non-atomic bigarray proxy creation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6344)), and a lock-free skiplist race ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6346)).
- [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened a run of CFG backend cleanups: a shared dead-code predicate ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6301](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6301)), `Cfg_loop_infos` improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6315](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6315)), reducibility checks in `Cfg_prologue.run` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6328)), and a new `[%%expect_asm_full]` codegen-test attribute paired with a stack-check/SIMD bug fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6334](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6334)).
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) opened further structured name-mangling work for binding operators and effect handlers ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6292)), a `Lazy` path item ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6303](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6303)), and functor instances ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6362)), following the merged `ocamlfilt` demangler ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)).
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a compiler-performance CI workflow benchmarking compile time on a corpus of real compiler source files ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6365](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6365)), with calibration PRs introducing a synthetic 2-3% slowdown ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6364](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6364)) and a no-op control PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6363)).
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) had a PR on instance type constructors in `typeopt` merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6113](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6113)) and then reverted by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) the same week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6343](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6343)).
- [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) merged a poison-value addition to Flambda 2 terms ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6273](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6273)) and opened a fix for continuations called with invalid kinds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6291)).
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) merged replacing several `Map.find` calls with `Map.find_or_null` in Flambda 2 for compilation speed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6290](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6290)) and opened a fix so Flambda 2 does not silently forget about bottom ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6347](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6347)).
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened inclusion of Merlin in the oxcaml compiler install ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6288](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6288)).
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened work to include oxcaml/merlin binaries in the oxcaml/oxcaml compiler install, coupling the two projects' release artifacts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6288](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6288)).
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened work integrating oxcaml/merlin's typing-recovery mechanism directly into the oxcaml/oxcaml type checker, aiming to reduce divergence between Merlin's and the compiler's type checkers ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6366](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6366)).
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) reported that `[@zero_alloc assume never_returns_normally]` is accepted by the checker on code that actually allocates when an inlined function raises an exception, and that plain `[@zero_alloc assume]` has the same unsoundness ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6297](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6297)); a related report showed the checker can also accept an allocating `raise_notrace` under `-Oclassic` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6261](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6261)).
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened a design discussion proposing to rework `[@inline]` attribute semantics, arguing the current meaning conflates cross-compilation-unit availability with automatic-inlining heuristics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6312](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6312)).
- On functional mode polymorphism, [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) flagged repeated instances of `with_zap_scope` resolving the zap scope before traversal runs, prompting fixes across `close_type`, `closed_extension_constructor`, `closed_class`, and `closed_type_decl` call sites ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483)).
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) reviewed the runtime-4 retirement commit-by-commit, questioning whether some mechanisms (e.g. `ocamlc -config` parsing behavior) should be preserved for diff-reduction with upstream OCaml ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- A large share of backend and debugging cleanup PRs this week carry a `100% vibe` label, spanning [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)'s CFG refactors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6301](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6301), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6302](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6302), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6315](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6315)) and [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)'s arm64 correctness fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6311](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6311), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6313](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6313)), indicating growing use of AI-assisted patches for mechanical backend hardening.
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened four runtime concurrency/race fixes in a single week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6332](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6332), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6342), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6344), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6346)), several found and drafted with AI assistance per the PR descriptions, following the runtime-4 retirement that simplified the runtime codebase ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
- Developer-tooling investment continued with the `ocamlfilt` demangler and its structured-mangling follow-ups ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6292), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6303](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6303), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6362)), alongside a new compiler-performance CI to catch regressions such as a synthetic 2-3% slowdown ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6363](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6363), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6364](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6364), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6365](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6365)).
- AVX512 backend support progressed as a multi-PR series from [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6293), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6294), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6339](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6339), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6340](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6340)), following the same incremental, split-PR pattern used for the `Allocation` mode axis by [@zhizhuoxin](https://github.com/zhizhuoxin) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6269), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6319](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6319)).

group_summaries.brief_summary: Twelve PRs merged in ocaml/ocaml covering codegen, GC, and cross-compilation fixes; ocaml/RFCs had no recorded activity this week.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Recursive-function codegen optimizations, RISC-V compressed instructions, native atomic fetch-add codegen, and typing/printast fixes landed.
group_summaries.new_features:
- Better compilation of **recursive function definitions** that avoids allocating pseudo-closure blocks for local aliases introduced before the function, addressing a code-generation regression reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876); implemented by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885)) and extended by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to cover redefinitions of identifiers from other modules ([ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894))
- **RISC-V** code generation now takes advantage of compressed instructions (the "C" ISA extension) when supported by the host processor, by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) ([ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873), [ocaml/ocaml#14799](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14799))
- Native code generation for `Atomic.fetch_and_add` (`Iatomic_fetch_add`) now emits direct assembly instead of going through a C call, extended to all remaining backends (amd64, arm64, riscv, power) by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575))
- Fixed an **assertion failure** in `out_type` when external types carry a manifest subject to module constraints, by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([ocaml/ocaml#14882](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14882), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14881](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14881))
- Fixed **mislabeled `Pmty_extension`** in the `-dparsetree` dump, which had incorrectly printed module type extensions with the `Pmod_extension` label, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([ocaml/ocaml#14880](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14880))
- Fixed a **runtime memory leak**: pthread attribute objects are now correctly destroyed after use, by [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) ([ocaml/ocaml#14875](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14875))
- Major GC now **adopts orphaned ephemerons early and often** (except when terminating), part of an ongoing ephemeron-simplification effort by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([ocaml/ocaml#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877), [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813))
- Fixed a **cross-compilation build failure** on older `mingw32-gcc` toolchains caused by a missing `errno.h` include, by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) ([ocaml/ocaml#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14883](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14883))
- More **concise printing of non-exhaustiveness warning counter-examples**, e.g. `A (_,_)` now prints as `A _`, by [Luc Maranget](https://github.com/maranget) ([ocaml/ocaml#14887](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14887))
- Expect tests now also run with `-rectypes` in addition to `-principal`, exercising recursive types in more of the test suite, by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([ocaml/ocaml#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/ocaml** was the only active repository in the group this week; 12 pull requests merged spanning code generation, garbage collection, and cross-compilation, alongside several typing bug reports and fixes
- **ocaml/RFCs** had no recorded activity during 2026-06-22 to 2026-06-28
- The week's work centered on performance: recursive-function code-generation optimizations addressing an inlining regression ([ocaml/ocaml#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876)), RISC-V compressed-instruction support ([ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873)), and continued ephemeron GC simplification ([ocaml/ocaml#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877), [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813))
- Several cross-compilation and Windows build-robustness fixes also merged ([ocaml/ocaml#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884), [ocaml/ocaml#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Static linking proposed; Windows cross-compilation, ephemeron GC, and recursive-function fixes merged; typing regressions triaged.
group_summaries.activity:
- Newly opened work includes **static and link-time linking** support for the compiler by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14895)), a fix for **pattern-matching switch overflow** on `abs min_int` by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml#14890](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14890)), rejection of a silently-dropped extension on module-dependent functor type domains by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([ocaml/ocaml#14886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14886)), an overflow guard for `caml_read_section_descriptors` during **bytecode loading** by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) ([ocaml/ocaml#14872](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14872)), further **ephemeron orphaning simplification** by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([ocaml/ocaml#14879](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14879)), and an update to the opam instructions in `HACKING.adoc` by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) ([ocaml/ocaml#14878](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14878))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a fix for a **scoping error in `ctype`** that erased result types when removing module dependencies from package types, closing [ocaml/ocaml#14891](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14891) reported by [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) ([ocaml/ocaml#14892](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14892))
- Merged work this week includes the **cross-compilation Windows fixes** ([ocaml/ocaml#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884), [ocaml/ocaml#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)), the `out_type`/external-types assertion fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14882](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14882)), the `Pmty_extension` printast fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14880](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14880)), the concise exhaustiveness-warning printing ([ocaml/ocaml#14887](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14887)), the recursive-function renaming optimizations ([ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885), [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894)), RISC-V compressed instructions ([ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873)), native `Iatomic_fetch_add` codegen ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)), the pthread attribute destruction fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14875](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14875)), the `-rectypes` expect-test coverage ([ocaml/ocaml#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)), and two ephemeron/GC PRs from [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s ongoing master plan ([ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745), [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [ocaml/ocaml#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877))
- Ongoing, previously-opened work still under review includes [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez)'s **new GC-pacing logic** ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s `Atomic.fold_update` proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14725](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14725)) and `Dynarray.unsafe_{get,set}` ([ocaml/ocaml#14864](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14864)), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)'s signature-inclusion alias handling ([ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)), [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse)'s configurable domain stack size ([ocaml/ocaml#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860)) and `Dynarray.sort` ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848)), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)'s instance-variable-lifting fix for [ocaml/ocaml#14861](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14861) ([ocaml/ocaml#14868](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14868)), [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)'s memory-model documentation for `memory.c` ([ocaml/ocaml#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857)), [Martin Constantino–Bodin](https://github.com/Mbodin)'s `Sys.mkdir` permissions documentation ([ocaml/ocaml#14863](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14863)), and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)'s small libunix fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14740](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14740))
- Two long-dormant PRs from 2021 by [Nicolas Chataing](https://github.com/nchataing) were closed without merging: adding `floatarray` manipulation functions ([ocaml/ocaml#10246](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10246)) and rewriting `get_unboxed_type_representation` with a terminating algorithm ([ocaml/ocaml#10479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10479))
- Typing regressions were reported and quickly triaged: [@yannl35133](https://github.com/yannl35133)'s report of a recursive first-class-module typing regression in 5.5 ([ocaml/ocaml#14888](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14888)) was closed as a duplicate/tracked issue, and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s related report ([ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630)) is being tracked by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) for a fix before the 5.6.0 release
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho)'s report of a **code-generation regression** in inlining of local closure variables since OCaml 5.2 ([ocaml/ocaml#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876)) prompted an extensive benchmarking discussion with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls), and [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) (who linked the related performance-regression thread [ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733)); benchmarks with and without flambda were compared before landing the fixes in [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885) and [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894)
- [@314eter](https://github.com/314eter)'s question about **operator precedence** in `with module type` constraints ([ocaml/ocaml#14874](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14874)) led [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) to discuss whether a breaking change is acceptable, converging on consistency with existing `with type` precedence; [@314eter](https://github.com/314eter) has a branch ready pending consensus
- [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann)'s proposal to add point-free `Option`/`Result` combinators `andthen`/`orelse` ([ocaml/ocaml#14893](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14893)) was clarified by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) to be distinct from the previously-discussed and closed [ocaml/ocaml#13937](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13937) proposal for `value_or`
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) continues a systematic, multi-PR **ephemeron orphaning/adoption simplification** effort tracked against a master plan in [ocaml/ocaml#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720), landing [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813) and [ocaml/ocaml#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877) this week and opening [ocaml/ocaml#14879](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14879) for further simplification
- Recursive-function and closure **code-generation optimization** work, triggered by the regression report in [ocaml/ocaml#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876), is proceeding incrementally with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) each contributing targeted renaming optimizations ([ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885), [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894))
- Continued investment in **cross-compilation and Windows build robustness**, spanning Windows toolchain fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850), [ocaml/ocaml#14884](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14884)), `BINDIR` overrides ([ocaml/ocaml#14869](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14869)), and small libunix fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14740](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14740))
- Multi-architecture **native code generation parity** work, extending atomic primitives and instruction selection across amd64, arm64, RISC-V, power, and s390x ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575), [ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Eio gained a portable Sockopt API for socket options while ocaml-uring merged vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Eio added a portable/Linux Sockopt API and relaxed Process.pipe types; ocaml-uring added vectored readv_fixed/writev_fixed bindings.
group_summaries.new_features:
- Added an extensible `Eio.Net.Sockopt` type with `Eio.Net.setsockopt`/`getsockopt`, a `register_printer` mechanism, and an `Invalid_option` error case, laying groundwork for typed socket options across all Eio backends, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) based on [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s original design ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575))
- Added portable socket-option constructors (`SO_REUSEADDR`, `SO_KEEPALIVE`, `TCP_NODELAY`, `SO_LINGER`, `SO_RCVTIMEO`, and others) usable even outside Unix processes, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), closing [ocaml-multicore/eio#322](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/322) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#376](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/376) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859))
- Added Linux-specific `TCP_*`/`IP_*` socket options (`TCP_CORK`, `TCP_KEEPIDLE`, `TCP_FASTOPEN`, `IP_FREEBIND`, `IP_MTU`, and others) implemented via `getsockopt`/`setsockopt` C stubs in `lib_eio_linux`, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575))
- Relaxed the return type of `Eio.Process.pipe` from a fixed closable-source/sink pair to a polymorphic-variant subtype, allowing use with libraries such as `Cohttp_eio.Body.t`, by [Isaac Arogbonlo](https://github.com/Arogbonlo) with casts by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), closing [ocaml-multicore/eio#750](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/750) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#775](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/775))
- Added `readv_fixed` and `writev_fixed` to the `Uring` API for multi-buffer reads/writes against a ring's registered fixed buffer, with `Invalid_argument` validation against buffers not belonging to the ring, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- Fixed misnamed C stubs (`submit_readv_fixed`/`submit_writev_fixed`, which never performed vectored I/O, renamed to `submit_read_fixed`/`submit_write_fixed`), freeing the vectored names for the new operations ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring@5669961](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/commit/56699618217cb588a1045ac43d1727e312544998))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Of the nine repositories in the multicore group, only [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) had recorded activity during 2026-06-22 to 2026-06-28
- [ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib), [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio), [ocaml-multicore/saturn](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/saturn), [ocaml-multicore/eio-trace](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio-trace), [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos), [ocaml-multicore/kcas](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/kcas), and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux) had no tracked commits, PRs, or issues this week
- Eio's work centered on a new portable/Linux socket-options API ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859), [ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575)) and relaxed typing for `Eio.Process.pipe` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#775](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/775))
- ocaml-uring merged vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)), while a related per-operation RWF-flags PR ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154)) and a liburing-vendoring issue ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)) remain open
group_summaries.activity_summary: Follow-on socket-option and RWF-flags PRs remain open, pty/login_tty work continues, and system-liburing vendoring is under discussion.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened `add SO_TYPE query to the portable Eio socket interface`, exploring whether querying a socket's type belongs in the portable `Sockopt` API ([ocaml-multicore/eio#860](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/860))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) closed the long-stale `eio_linux: add support for integration with Async` draft, citing lack of continued interest ([ocaml-multicore/eio#221](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/221))
- The pseudoterminal (`Eio_unix.Pty`) and `login_tty` fork-action work by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) continued, with a FreeBSD-specific test failure (pty buffers discarded on close, unlike Linux) diagnosed with shell access from [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers), plus design discussion with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on re-exporting `Unix.tcgetattr` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531))
- The per-operation RWF flags PR by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), exposing `preadv2`/`pwritev2`-style flags such as `dsync` and `nocache` at the uring layer, remains open; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) asked for a rebase after the fixed-buffer bindings merged, and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) reviewed the changes favorably ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154))
- Discussion continues on whether ocaml-uring should support building against system `liburing` instead of only the vendored copy, following a Debian build failure caused by a missing `io_uring_prep_readv_fixed` declaration ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The open per-operation RWF-flags PR in ocaml-uring ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154)) exposes `preadv2`/`pwritev2`-style flags explicitly intended for later use in Eio_linux, directly linking the two repositories' roadmaps
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) were both active as authors and reviewers across [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) this week, reflecting the shared low-level I/O stack between Eio's Linux backend and the uring bindings it depends on
- Eio's new Linux-specific socket options ([ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575)) and ocaml-uring's fixed-buffer vectored I/O ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)) both extend typed OCaml coverage of the same underlying Linux kernel interfaces that `lib_eio_linux` consumes
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) discussed whether ocaml-uring should support building against system `liburing` rather than only a vendored copy, after Debian's patched build failed with an implicit-declaration error for `io_uring_prep_readv_fixed`; opam-installed `uring` often needs a newer `liburing` than stable distributions ship ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) debated whether Eio's portable `Sockopt` API should expose `SO_TYPE` as a raw integer, agreeing to omit it until it can be mapped to a proper variant type rather than an unhelpful platform-dependent integer ([ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- Both [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) continued building typed, portable OCaml wrappers around raw Unix/kernel interfaces — Eio's socket-options and pty/`login_tty` work ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859), [ocaml-multicore/eio#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531)) and ocaml-uring's fixed-buffer vectored I/O ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155)) — rather than requiring users to drop to raw syscalls
- Large single-author PRs are being split during review to ease landing, as when [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) divided [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s `Sockopt` PR ([ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575)) into a base API PR ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858)) and a portable-options PR ([ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859))
- Packaging/distribution tension around vendored vs. system dependencies surfaced in ocaml-uring's liburing-vendoring discussion ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)), as newer kernel-feature bindings require `liburing` versions ahead of what stable distributions package
- Seven of the nine group repositories ([ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib), [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio), [ocaml-multicore/saturn](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/saturn), [ocaml-multicore/eio-trace](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio-trace), [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos), [ocaml-multicore/kcas](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/kcas), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux)) had no recorded activity this week, concentrating all visible work in eio and ocaml-uring

weekly_summary.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5 support shipped in merlin and ocaml-lsp releases while oxcaml retired its OCaml-4 runtime and eio gained a portable Sockopt API.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Merlin v5.8-505 and ocaml-lsp 1.27.0 released with OCaml 5.5 support; oxcaml added -experimental-optimizations; eio shipped Sockopt API.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) published release **v5.8-505** with **OCaml 5.5** support, by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077), [release](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.8-505)), bundling a signature-help fix for type aliases ([ocaml/merlin#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067)) and a locate fix for punned let bindings ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) published release **1.27.0**, adding **OCaml 5.5** support ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609), [release](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/releases/tag/1.27.0)) and no longer forcing Merlin's `real_paths = false`, letting full type-alias paths show in errors, fixed by [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1579), closes [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1395))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) added command/subcommand/flag completion for bash, zsh, and pwsh via `dune completion [shell]`, by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) ([ocaml/dune#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14779)), and made `dune build --auto-promote`/`dune fmt` exit 0 on success, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#15284](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15284))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) added a native `OpamTar` module reading/writing tar.gz archives via the `tar` library instead of shelling out, by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) building on work by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged new `Crypto` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380)) and `Notification`/`Clipboard` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2379](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2379)) Web API bindings, and `Lwt_js_events.mutation`/`mutations` for `MutationObserver` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2376](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2376)), all by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) added a new `-experimental-optimizations` compiler flag bundling off-by-default codegen improvements (CFG prologue shrink-wrap, x86 peephole, `irc` allocator, CFG block merging), by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6174](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6174))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) removed the legacy OCaml-4 runtime and dual-runtime switch from [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)), and [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) landed `ocamlfilt`, a demangler for compiler symbol names ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) added an extensible `Eio.Net.Sockopt` API with portable socket-option constructors (`SO_REUSEADDR`, `TCP_NODELAY`, and others) and Linux-specific `TCP_*`/`IP_*` options, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) based on [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s original design ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859), [ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/575))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) added `readv_fixed`/`writev_fixed` for multi-buffer reads/writes against registered fixed buffers, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) improved compilation of **recursive function definitions** to avoid allocating pseudo-closure blocks for local aliases, fixing a code-generation regression ([ocaml/ocaml#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876)), by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885)) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **RISC-V** code generation now uses compressed instructions (the "C" ISA extension) when supported, by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) ([ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873)); native codegen for `Atomic.fetch_and_add` was extended to all remaining backends by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) added four new packages including **soteria** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30150](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30150)) and **playwright** OCaml bindings ([ocaml/opam-repository#30146](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30146)), released **dune 3.24.0** across 18 packages by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)), added the **OCaml 5.5.0** manual by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095)), and shipped **melange 7.0.0** by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30094](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30094))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)'s `watch.ocaml.org` video scraper now catches per-source fetch failures as a non-fatal error instead of aborting the whole Planet scrape job, by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged 12 pull requests spanning code generation, garbage collection, and cross-compilation, centered on recursive-function codegen optimizations ([ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885), [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894)), RISC-V compressed instructions ([ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873)), and continued ephemeron GC simplification by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([ocaml/ocaml#14877](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14877)); [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened a proposal for **static and link-time linking** support ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14895))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was the only active repository in the group, retiring the OCaml-4 runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)) and adding the `-experimental-optimizations` flag ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6174](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6174)); [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) opened a multi-PR **AVX512** backend effort ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6293), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6339](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6339)) and [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened four runtime concurrency/race fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6332](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6332), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6342), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6344), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6346))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Only [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) had recorded activity among the group's nine repositories; eio's work centered on a portable/Linux **Sockopt** API ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859)) while ocaml-uring merged vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) had a high-volume week with four new packages, **dune 3.24.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)), and **OCaml 5.5.0** ecosystem readiness ([ocaml/opam-repository#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095), [ocaml/opam-repository#30117](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30117)); [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) fixed a `watch.ocaml.org` scraper outage ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3695)) and had five content PRs pending merge
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) released **v5.8-505** and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) released **1.27.0**, both adding **OCaml 5.5** support; [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ran an extended RPC/watch-mode refactor led by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#15345](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15345), [ocaml/dune#15346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15346)), and [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) advanced its tar-based repository-format rewrite ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6625))

multicore: -Only [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) had recorded activity among the group's nine repositories; eio's work centered on a portable/Linux **Sockopt** API ([ocaml-multicore/eio#858](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/858), [ocaml-multicore/eio#859](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/859)) while ocaml-uring merged vectored fixed-buffer read/write bindings ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/155))

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Recursive-function codegen work, oxcaml's runtime-4 retirement fallout, eio/uring follow-ons, and dune's RPC refactor advanced across groups.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ Typing regressions were triaged, including [@yannl35133](https://github.com/yannl35133)'s report of a recursive first-class-module regression in 5.5 ([ocaml/ocaml#14888](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14888)) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s related report ([ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630)), tracked by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) for a fix before **5.6.0**; two long-dormant PRs from 2021 by [Nicolas Chataing](https://github.com/nchataing) were closed without merging ([ocaml/ocaml#10246](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10246), [ocaml/ocaml#10479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10479))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened a run of CFG backend cleanups ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6301](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6301), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6315](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6315), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6328)), [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) extended structured name-mangling for binding operators, effect handlers, and functor instances ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6292), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6362](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6362)), and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a compiler-performance CI workflow ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6365](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6365))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s pseudoterminal (`Eio_unix.Pty`) and `login_tty` fork-action work continued, with a FreeBSD-specific test failure diagnosed by [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/531)); the per-operation RWF-flags PR exposing `preadv2`/`pwritev2`-style flags remains open pending rebase ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The dune 3.24.0 release forced immediate downstream upper-bound pins on cppo, lablgtk3, wasm_of_ocaml-compiler ([ocaml/opam-repository#30143](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30143)) and camomile ([ocaml/opam-repository#30132](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30132)); [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) recovered several packages' source tarballs into opam-source-archives after upstream links broke ([ocaml/opam-repository#30129](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30129), [ocaml/opam-repository#30126](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30126))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) continued iterating on an **LRU cache for indexing memory usage** in merlin ([ocaml/merlin#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)); [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache bindings for js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381)); [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) began reproducing an odoc_driver sibling-library module-resolution report for Eliom ([ocaml/odoc#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450))

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OCaml 5.5 rollout**: support merged in lockstep across [ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077) (released as v5.8-505) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1609) (released as 1.27.0), with [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) resyncing its CI/test matrix the same week ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2374](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2374))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository)'s **OCaml 5.5.0** manual package ([ocaml/opam-repository#30095](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30095)) shipped the same week as matching **merlin** and **ocaml-lsp-server** 5.5 support ([ocaml/opam-repository#30117](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30117)), and [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)'s automated platform-release scrape structurally depends on releases such as **dune 3.24.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) and **melange 7.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30094](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30094))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened work to include **oxcaml/merlin** binaries in the oxcaml/oxcaml compiler install ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6288](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6288)), and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened work integrating merlin's typing-recovery mechanism into the oxcaml type checker to reduce divergence between the two ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6366](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6366))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) were active as authors and reviewers across both [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), reflecting the shared low-level I/O stack; the open per-operation RWF-flags PR in ocaml-uring ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/154)) is intended for later use in Eio_linux
- __RUMINANT:tools__ A coordinated Dependabot `actions/checkout` v6→v7 bump landed the same week across [ocaml/merlin#2081](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2081), [ocaml/odoc#1451](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1451), [ocaml/opam#6969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6969), and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2368](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2368), also mirrored in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3691)

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho)'s report of a **code-generation regression** in inlining of local closure variables since OCaml 5.2 ([ocaml/ocaml#14876](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14876)) prompted an extensive benchmarking discussion with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls), and [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) before the fixes landed in [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14885) and [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14894)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) reported that `[@zero_alloc assume never_returns_normally]` is accepted by the checker on code that actually allocates when an inlined function raises, an unsoundness also present in plain `[@zero_alloc assume]` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6297](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6297)); [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) separately opened a design discussion proposing to rework `[@inline]` attribute semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6312](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6312))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) discussed whether ocaml-uring should support building against system `liburing` rather than only a vendored copy, after Debian's patched build failed with an implicit-declaration error ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ A bad checksum on the **wol** packages was traced to a change in how the tangled.org forge generates git-archive tarballs; [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported it and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) discussed publishing a static, annotated-tag archive instead ([ocaml/opam-repository#30089](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30089))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) identified the likely root cause of a dune-vs-ocaml-lsp ppx error discrepancy as mismatched working directories for ppx execution ([ocaml/merlin#2082](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2082)), and [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) described how odoc_driver resolves same-named sibling-library modules to the wrong library for Eliom's client/server split ([ocaml/odoc#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450))

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:core__ Multi-architecture **native code generation parity** work continued, extending atomic primitives and instruction selection across amd64, arm64, RISC-V, and power ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575), [ocaml/ocaml#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14873)), alongside [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s systematic **ephemeron orphaning/adoption simplification** effort ([ocaml/ocaml#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ A large share of backend and debugging cleanup PRs this week carry a `100% vibe` label, and several of [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes)'s runtime concurrency fixes note AI assistance in their descriptions, following the runtime-4 retirement that simplified the runtime codebase ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) continued building typed, portable OCaml wrappers around raw Unix/kernel interfaces rather than requiring users to drop to raw syscalls, with seven of the group's nine repositories having no recorded activity this week
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Dune version-bound fallout** and **recurring archive breakage** continued as recurring maintenance themes in opam-repository, while **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem readiness** (manual, merlin, ocaml-lsp-server) shipped in the same week as noted in [week 25](data/summaries/weekly/week-25-2026.json)'s compiler release
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Shell-outs replaced by native OCaml** continued as a theme, with opam's tar rewrite ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6945)) and js_of_ocaml's growing native Web API binding surface ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2380](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2380), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2381)), plus continued consolidation of dune's RPC/watch-mode logic into the core build system
